redefine the <em>'message'</em> element (enclosed in single quotation marks), and specify the message text (enclosed in double quotation marks). </li><li data-uuid="09fc3020-996b-40e7-b81b-1d526f4d00bf"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0ec68cb-d654-4605-9a54-6dd7fc20e3bc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomValidationSuite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomValidationSuite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Validate Part Usage Type'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">templateRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">severity</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">severity::warning</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">effect</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">//  specifying the validation rule's message</span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'message'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Part usage must be defined only by part definition.&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></u><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Specifying the validation rule condition</h3><p>To specify the validation rule condition</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="dca2ac9f-0aaf-4f68-bf10-4f7eb31d7fa8">Within the body of the part usage element of the new validation rule, declare a part usage and redefine the <em>condition </em>element.</li><li data-uuid="2287aca5-79bd-4288-a063-27f4111910d3">Within the body of the condition's part usage, do the following:<ol><li data-uuid="659aa34a-d967-43cf-a706-9ae8eb120639">(Optional) Declare a <em>ref item</em>, redefine <em>subjectType</em>, and specify the element metaclasses the validation should check. Target metaclasses with metadata access expression.<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5696c617-c14e-43bc-b3a0-499621547032"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="a12a1151-2ec4-42fd-a617-a7014bb2f48c">List multiple metaclasses by enclosing them in parentheses and separating them with commas, e.g., <em>ref item :&gt;&gt; subjectType = (SysML::Systems::PartUsage.metadata, SysML::Systems::ActionUsage.metadata)</em>;</li><li data-uuid="b966a4d0-0c09-4b31-b0bb-4c7cd0ad824d">If you do not specify the <em>subjectType,</em> the validation rule applies to all elements and should be narrowed down via the expression specification for the calculation (see the next step).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="cabc972b-7a4c-4a93-9f96-56469378b820">Declare a calculation usage and redefine the <em>test </em>element. Within the calc's body, specify the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the validation rule's condition. <br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eebeaf79-142b-4712-abd7-7223b95538f0"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f3b7bfca-8d3e-4c05-adac-c753f57b2b79">Use functions to construct the condition expression. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Functions" /></ac:link> subpages containing predefined functions with examples and explanations.</li><li data-uuid="2620fe8b-2254-4952-9b1d-6663439607fe">You can also use the SysML v2 Open API for the creation of validation rule condition expressions. These expressions should be written in Java, compiled, and specified in the modeling tool's classpath. You can then specify these expressions as validation rule expressions in the model. For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Creating Custom Validation Rules and Suites" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05f34aaa-2434-488b-8493-2a8f486f3a7a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">   </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">test</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">      </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">rep</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> script </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">language</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;JavaMethod&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* specify the name of the class with the needed expression */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">   }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="619d97b9-3806-497d-bb5b-e9a60b94a8f9"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae3ca780-e28d-4998-b21e-534f1adbbf4e"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0caa7b1a-42b7-4d3e-b6dc-bb198b4f78d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="468a465c-e511-41a6-983d-8084d95cdb2f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomValidationSuite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomValidationSuite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Validate Part Usage Feature Typing'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">templateRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">severity</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">severity::error</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">effect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'message'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Part usage must be defined only by part definition.&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying a subjectType, then using a simpler expression in the calculation:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> { </span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">ref</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">item</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">subjectType</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">metadata</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span></u><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">test</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                    <u>(</u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Feature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">type</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">hastype</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartDefinition</span></u><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">} </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2f6f849-d9c7-4201-bd50-9b1301c9e5ec"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The first option uses the <em>subjectType to </em>narrow down evaluable elements to part usages. The calculation's expression:<br /><em>(element <ac:link ac:anchor="as"><ri:page ri:content-title="Base Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[as]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> KerML::Feature).type <ac:link ac:anchor="hastype"><ri:page ri:content-title="Base Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[hastype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> SysML::Systems::PartDefinition</em> - targets the part usage's type (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Feature typing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[feature typing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), then checks whether it is a part definition. If it is, the condition evaluates to <em>true</em> (validation passed); if it is not, the condition evaluates to <em>false</em> (validation failed). An element fails the validation rule if the condition expression evaluates to <em>false</em>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b341ec36-f368-4cc1-bf0a-490b3c20b5fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f841d113-c1fc-4d27-a1ae-74acedcb16d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomValidationSuite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomValidationSuite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_Validation::CoreValidationComponents::Suite</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Validate Part Usage Feature Typing'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">templateRule</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">severity</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">severity::error</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">effect</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'message'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> = </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Part usage must be defined only by part definition.&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// not specifying a subjectType and using a complete expression in the calculation:</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">condition</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">calc</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">test</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                    </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">not</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">hastype</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartUsage</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">) </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">or</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> (</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">element</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">as</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">KerML::Feature</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">).</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">type</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">hastype</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">SysML::Systems::PartDefinition</span></u><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">                }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">} </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba92c966-115d-4cc9-9c5d-b00940feeecf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The second option discards <em>subjectType</em> and uses an expression to do the same:</p><p><em><ac:link ac:anchor="not"><ri:page ri:content-title="Boolean Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[not]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (element <ac:link ac:anchor="hastype"><ri:page ri:content-title="Base Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[hastype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> SysML::Systems::PartUsage)</em> - checks if an element is not a part usage. If the element is not a part usage, the condition evaluates to <em>true</em> (validation passed); if the element is a part usage, the condition evaluates to <em>false</em> (validation failed).</p><p><em>(element <ac:link ac:anchor="as"><ri:page ri:content-title="Base Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[as]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> KerML::Feature).type <ac:link ac:anchor="hastype"><ri:page ri:content-title="Base Functions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[hastype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> SysML::Systems::PartDefinition</em> - targets the part usage's type (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Feature typing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[feature typing]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>), then checks whether it is a part definition. If it is, the condition evaluates to <em>true</em> (validation passed); if it is not, the condition evaluates to <em>false</em> (validation failed).</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="or"><ri:page ri:content-title="Control Functions" /><ac:link-body>The <em>or</em> function</ac:link-body></ac:link> checks both conditions; if either one of the conditions, or both, evaluates to <em>true</em>, then it returns <em>true</em> (validation passed). Thus, if only one of the conditions evaluates to <em>false</em>, the outcome is still <em>true</em>, meaning the validation rule is not failed and the element passes the validation. The element only fails the validation if it evaluates to <em>false</em> for both conditions.</p><p>For example, if the evaluated element is:</p><ul><li data-uuid="ec0b5df8-0363-4f77-b073-73a9d8f873b4">a part usage that is defined by a part definition → first condition: (<em>false</em>) failed, second condition: (<em>true</em>) passed, outcome: validation passed.</li><li data-uuid="b2fb300f-eabd-4895-ad5b-525a899e3563">an action usage that is defined by an action definition → first condition: (<em>true</em>) passed, because the first condition passed, the second is not evaluated, outcome: validation passed.</li><li data-uuid="01d7a506-d48c-43af-852b-d67ea1d6b4c3">a part usage that is defined by an action definition → first condition: (<em>false</em>) failed, second condition: (<em>false</em>) failed, outcome: validation failed.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577902 space=SYSML2P version=32 -->
## PAGE 00550: Creating View Creation dialog

- page_id: `249577902`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577902/Creating+View+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 32
- version_date: `2026-02-09T14:31:48.545+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model customization > Custom creation dialogs > Custom View Creation dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for custom View Creation dialog and submenu creation.

#### NOTE: Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Updating the View Creation dialog/submenu

Once you have created the new View Creation dialog/submenu, activate it via the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating View Creation dialog' space=''].

The default name for the View Creation dialog's invocation command is **Create View**. If you create a dialog from scratch and do not [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing View Creation dialog' space=''], the default name remains.

##### Creating a custom View Creation dialog/submenu

To create a custom View Creation dialog/submenu

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, subclassify the ViewCreationDialog definition from the DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs package, then, within the body of the definition:
  1. 
  2. 
5. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomViewCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{// naming the new dialog instance and subclassifying the*ViewCreationDialog* 
} 
}

##### Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

###### Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

To create view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, declare a part usage, name it (optional), then subset the itemCategories element.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{partcustomSysMLViewsCategory:>itemCategories; // adding a command category} 
}

###### Specifying the view creation command category attributes for the View Creation dialog/submenu

To specify the view creation command category attributes for the View Creation dialog/submenu

1. Place the cursor within the body of the new command category's element and redefine the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefNewCustomViewCreationDialog:> DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{partcustomSysMLViewsCategory:>itemCategories {attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue; // specifying the command category's default collapse state 
attribute:>>labeldefault"SysML v2 Symbolic Views"; // specifying the command category's name}}}

##### Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu commands

You can customize the View Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which view creation commands it should contain. The view creation commands themselves are created by targeting view templates (e.g., *generalViewTemplate*, etc.). There can be two types of templates:

- predefined templates . These templates are available via the DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates , which contains predefined templates for all standard predefined view definitions ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbolic views' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tabular views' space=''] ).
- custom templates . You need to for any custom-created view definitions (e.g., [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''] ) if you want their corresponding view creation commands to be available in the View Creation dialog/submenu.

###### Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem and subset abstractItems . This element instantiates the new command.
2. Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the label attribute with the new view creation command name .
3. Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate and redefine operation. In the element's body, specify the in direction based on the view template you want to use for the command using a metaclassification expression. Alternatively, you can also use *MetadataAccessExpression*, e.g., when the templated view is named. See the textual notation example below.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomViewCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomViewCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ViewCreationDialog{ 
partcustomSysMLViewsCategory:>itemCategories{

// view creation command by targeting a custom view template: 
// (metaclassification expression is used to target an unnamed templated element in a package) 
partreqViewItem:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{// new view creation command instance 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Custom Requirements View";// specifying the command's name 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{// specifying the view template that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command 
inref= (customRequirementsViewTemplatemetaKerML::Package).ownedElement;// the specified template is an unnamed custom view template, see the procedure 'Creating view templates for custom templated view creation commands' 
} 
} 
 
// view creation command by targeting a custom view template: 
// (*MetadataAccessExpression* is used to target a named templated element) 
partreqViewItem2:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{// new view creation command instance 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Custom Requirements View 2";// specifying the command's name 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{// specifying the view template that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command 
inref='Custom Requirements View 2'.metadata;// the specified template is a named custom view template, see the procedure 'Creating view templates for custom templated view creation commands' 
} 
} 
 
// view creation command by targeting a predefined view template: 
partgvButton:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{// new view creation command instance 
attribute:>>labeldefault"General View";// specifying the command's name 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{// specifying the view template that should be targeted upon the clicking of the new command 
inref= (DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates::generalViewTemplatemetaKerML::Package).ownedElement;// the specified template is a predefined view template from DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates 
} 
} 
 
} 
} 
}

###### Creating view templates for custom templated view creation commands

To create view templates for custom templated view creation commands

1. Within the body of the new dialog instance's package, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] for the custom view template.
2. Within the package, declare a view usage defined by the custom view definition.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packagecustomRequirementsViewTemplate{ // the new template's package, which you will have to target using the*OperationFromTemplate*to create a view creation command for the custom element; use metaclassification expression to target it 
 view:CustomRequirementsView::'Requirements View';// the new unnamed templated element (a view defined by elsewhere declared *CustomRequirementsView::'Requirements View')* 
}

view'Custom Requirements View 2':CustomRequirementsView::'Requirements View'; // the new named templated element (a view defined by elsewhere declared *CustomRequirementsView::'Requirements View');*use the *MetadataAccessExpression*to target it

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// specifying the command category's name</span><br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">      }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Managing the View Creation dialog/submenu commands</h3><p>You can customize the View Creation dialog/submenu content by specifying which view creation commands it should contain. The view creation commands themselves are created by targeting view templates (e.g., <em>generalViewTemplate</em>, etc.). There can be two types of templates:</p><ul><li data-uuid="f48c3bf8-d50e-4497-af35-feb8c537ebde"><strong>predefined templates</strong>. These templates are available via the <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::ViewsTemplates</em>, which contains predefined templates for all standard predefined view definitions (<ac:link ac:anchor="Symbolic views"><ri:page ri:content-title="Symbolic views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[symbolic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link ac:anchor="Tabular views"><ri:page ri:content-title="Tabular views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tabular]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>). </li><li data-uuid="38502b66-9b46-4a90-b94a-5e229e0d1f56"><strong>custom templates</strong>. You need to <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating view templates for custom templated view creation commands "><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create custom view templates]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for any custom-created view definitions (e.g., <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[custom symbolic view definitions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) if you want their corresponding view creation commands to be available in the View Creation dialog/submenu.</li></ul><h4>Adding view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To add view creation commands to the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="e1a153c2-36de-441e-bb64-6475df3cf7b1">Within the body of the command category's element, declare a part usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem</em> and subset <em>abstractItems</em>. This element instantiates the new command.</li><li data-uuid="3a596508-f535-4935-b526-9fbe872a96ab">Within the body of the new command's instance, specify the command's name by redefining the <em>label </em>attribute with the new view creation command name<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7a163c04-f4d1-4e5d-b7dc-64011aeb046d">.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li data-uuid="69506dff-f552-48fc-8bf8-329724ebe451">Within the body of the new command's instance, declare a perform action usage defined by <em>DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate</em> and redefine <em>operation. </em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the element's body, specify the </span><em>in</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> direction </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">based on the view template you want to use for the command using a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="f9cb8149-6279-469c-b31c-58060532684a">metaclassification expression</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ca0c5fa-5676-455f-b7a1-716295f8e7b9"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Alternatively, you can also use <em>MetadataAccessExpression</em>, e.g., <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="68d0b09a-c68f-4a58-8353-d7925e8f234f">when the templated view is named. See the textual notation example below.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="ec9c9f24-cda3-4d7f-b72c-e5ba16797a36"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d99a8172-fc34-448c-b498-1aa6696c6e9c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span templates for custom templated view creation commands </p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="df35a4ac-822e-469c-bcf1-310e2a9e296e">Within the body of the new dialog instance's package, <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a package element via the Textual Editor"><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a new package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the custom view template.</li><li data-uuid="470ab27b-c7c7-4eec-b87c-5f53ee3d47b0">Within the package, declare a view usage defined by the custom view definition.</li><li data-uuid="6a2feee6-9bee-4981-85df-c3df91277af2"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6552abf0-5716-48ec-b12c-41d20ff5fc0b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">customRequirementsViewTemplate</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the new template's package, which you will have to target using the<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">OperationFromTemplate</em><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span> </span>to create a view creation command for the custom element; use metaclassification expression to target it</span></span><br />    <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRequirementsView::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Requirements View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;  </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the new unnamed templated element (a view defined by elsewhere declared <em>CustomRequirementsView::'Requirements View')</em></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">view</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'Custom Requirements View 2'</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomRequirementsView::</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Requirements View'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// the new named templated element <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">(a view defined by elsewhere declared <em>CustomRequirementsView::'Requirements View'); </em>use the <em>MetadataAccessExpression </em>to target it</span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

## PAGE 00551: Creating views

- page_id: `249577724`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577724/Creating+views

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Procedures for creating views depend on the view kind. See the table below and choose the appropriate page.

| --- | --- | --- |

|  |  |  |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577449 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00552: Cross features

- page_id: `249577449`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577449/Cross+features
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:29:47.864+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Structure > Connection
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A cross feature allows you to linkthe value at one end of the connection with the value at the other end.It imposes a semantic constraint ensuring that the connected elements are related via a defined reference path in the model.Cross features can be specified for ends of certain connectors, such as[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connection' space='']or[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface' space=''].

Cross features can be specified for connector definition or usage ends. There are two options for specifying them:

1. Declaring cross features in the related types of the connector definition (using cross subsetting).
2. Declaring cross features in connector ends (owned cross features), options:
  1. Notation:
    1. Full notation
    2. Shorthand notation (only for connector usages)
  2. Named or unnamed

Cross feature specification includes cross multiplicity, which constrains the number of connections, rather than end values. Unlike regular multiplicity, cross multiplicity is used only within the context of cross features and is usually specified for the *end*elements of a connector. For end elements, cross multiplicity is specified after the *end* keyword, followed by the ordering and/or uniqueness for at least one of the ends.

Please note that cross features is the correct mechanism to specify multiplicities of the connection definitions and usages while connection end multiplicities are fixed to [1] in SysML v2.0. The multiplicities that were specified on the association ends in SysML v1.x models have to be specified on cross features and not on the connection ends when porting models to SysML v2.0.

##### Specifying cross features for connector ends

###### Specifying cross features for connector ends via the Textual Editor

Specifying cross features for connector ends via the Textual Editor

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Declare cross features in the related types of the connector usage/definition:
    1. Specify cross multiplicity and ordering for the related types.
    2. Specify the connector usage's/definition's end features to subset cross features owned by the related type. Cross subsettingIdentify the usage element that you want to be the subsetting one.Follow the subsetting element with either one of the following:keyword*crosses*textual symbol*=>*Follow with a feature chain in which the first feature is the otherconnection end and the second feature is the cross feature for that end.Alternatively, you can also perform the procedure via the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] in a view or the Containment tree.
  2. Declare cross features for ends:
    1. Full notation:
      1. (Optional) Specify the cross feature name.
      2. Specify the kind keyword for the ends.
      3. Specify cross multiplicity, ordering, and/or uniqueness on at least one of the ends. Optionally, specify a cross feature name after the end keyword.
    2. Shorthand notation (available only for connector usages):
      1. Declare the *connection***keyword, specify the element name (optional) and (optional) . State the features explicitly (using the connect...to syntax ) or in a comma-separated list of related features prefixed with cross multiplicity. With shorthand notation, cross features in ends do not accept names; ends do not accept ordering.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

50%

/* ------------ connection definition ------------ */

/* cross features owned by the end type (uses cross subsetting); Step 1.a */

partdefHub{ 
refpartconnectedDevices[1..*]ordered:Device; 
} 
partdefDevice{ 
refpartconnectingHub[0..1] :Hub; 
} 
connectiondefDeviceConnection{ 
/* unnamed cross features in ends */ 
endparthub:Hub=>device.connectingHub; // the kind keyword *part*is optional 
endpartdevice:Device=>hub.connectedDevices; 
}

/* ------------ connection usage ------------ */

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*]; 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnection{

/* cross features owned by the end type (uses cross subsetting) */ 
endhub::>networkHubs=>device.connectingHub; 
enddevice::>networkDevices=>hub.connectedDevices;}}

50%

/* ------------ connection definition ------------ */

/* cross features owned by the ends; Step 1.b */

partdefHub; 
partdefDevice;

// named cross features in ends:

connectiondefDeviceConnection{endcrossHub[0..1]parthub:Hub; // the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
endcrossDevice[1..*]orderedpartdevice:Device; 
}

// unnamed cross features in ends:

connection def DeviceConnection { 
 end [0..1] part hub : Hub;// the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
 end [1..*] ordered part device : Device; 
}

/* ------------ connection usage ------------ */

// named cross features owned by ends (full notation):

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*]; 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnection{

/* cross features owned by ends; cross features named in ends (full notation) */ 
endcrossHub[1]parthub::>networkHubs; // the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
endcrossDevice [0..4]orderedpartdevice::>networkDevices; 
}}

// unnamed cross features owned by ends (full notation):

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*]; 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnection{

/* cross features owned by ends; cross features unnamed in ends (full notation) */ 
end[1]parthub::>networkHubs; // the kind keyword (*part)*is mandatory 
end[0..4]orderedpartdevice::>networkDevices;

}}

// cross features owned by ends (shorthands):

partdefNetworkConfiguration{ 
partnetworkHubs:Hub[*]; 
partnetworkDevices:Device[*];

/* cross features owned by ends (shorthands) */ 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnectionconnect[1]hub::>networkHubsto[0..4]device::>networkDevices; 
// OR (equivalent to the above) 
connectionnetworkConnections:DeviceConnectionconnect([1]hub::>networkHubs,[0..4]device::>networkDevices); 
}

##### Moving cross features between end types and ends

Although there are two options for specifying cross features for ends, you can easily refactor connector definition ends from one option to the other.

To move cross features between end types and ends

- Right-click the end of a connector definition and in the shortcut menu, select Refactor , then click one of the following:
  - **Move Cross Feature to End** to move cross features declared in the related types of the connector definition to the connector ends.
  - Move Cross Feature to End Type to move cross features declared in the connector ends to the related types of the connector definition. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A cross feature allows you to link </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">the value at one end of the connection with the value at the other end. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">It imposes a semantic constraint ensuring that the connected elements are related via a defined reference path in the model. </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Cross features can be specified for ends of certain connectors, such as </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[connection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> or </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Cross features can be specified for connector definition or usage ends. There are two options for specifying them:</span></p><ol><li data-uuid="7cf4314e-c11e-4203-a785-a385b059b2ad">Declaring cross features in the related types of the connector definition (using cross subsetting).</li><li data-uuid="d24eefa0-336d-44c5-bfe9-e8649f98b434">Declaring cross features in connector ends (owned cross features), options:<ol><li data-uuid="3651ff9d-8d69-43df-8851-227cc15170e4">Notation:<ol><li data-uuid="c0d2cd1a-a1cf-4b6d-8db7-36098aa240a9">Full notation</li><li data-uuid="83f6d88a-16e7-4056-96db-ca43cb23a9e6">Shorthand notation (only for connector usages)</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="d5f44d45-b4e4-46ba-b4dc-1527b149aa55">Named or unnamed</li></ol></li></ol><p>Cross feature specification includes cross multiplicity, which constrains the number of connections, rather than end values. Unlike regular multiplicity, cross multiplicity is used only within the context of cross features and is usually specified for the <em>end </em>elements of a connector. For end elements, cross multiplicity is specified after the <em>end</em> keyword, followed by the ordering and/or uniqueness for at least one of the ends. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e8947f7-ae87-46fe-ab7b-1c3872e20e7d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please note that cross features is the correct mechanism to specify multiplicities of the connection definitions and usages while connection end multiplicities are fixed to [1] in SysML v2.0. The multiplicities that were specified on the association ends in SysML v1.x models have to be specified on cross features and not on the connection ends when porting models to SysML v2.0.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 style="text-align: left;">Specifying cross features for connector ends</h3><h4 style="text-align: left;">Specifying cross features for connector ends via the Textual Editor</h4><p>Specifying cross features for connector ends via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="6b4b5bd1-a452-4eeb-ac37-cc3d8595c865">Do one of the following:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li>Declare cross features in the related types of the connector usage/definition:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li>Specify cross multiplicity and ordering for the related types. </li><li>Specify the connector usage's/definition's end features to subset cross features owned by the related type.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6daba5d8-e267-4eb3-95e0-583d541a7764"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Cross subsetting</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="40057937-a13b-4d6e-ba21-88284be46787">Identify the usage element that you want to be the subsetting one.</li><li data-uuid="0165e300-aa80-4b8e-b827-aeb978215fbc">Follow the subsetting element with either one of the following:<ol><li>keyword<em><span> </span>crosses</em></li><li>textual symbol<span> <em>=&gt;</em></span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="ce50fb7c-8dc8-45bd-b60a-b1e7c66596ce"><p class="auto-cursor-target">Follow with a <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">feature chain in which the first feature is the other </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">connection end and the second feature is the cross feature for that end.</span></p></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Alternatively, you can also perform the procedure via the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating a relation via the Create Relation command"><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[cross subset Create Relation command]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in a view or the Containment tree. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>Declare cross features for ends:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="a660324c-e86a-474f-b69f-7eac8e71f02e">Full notation:<ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="121984d0-07f5-4b44-84cf-5d6b71dd844a">(Optional) Specify the cross feature name.</li><li data-uuid="555ee742-0008-45e6-93a9-0ad188eae0b8">Specify the kind keyword for the ends.</li><li>Specify cross multiplicity, ordering, and/or uniqueness on at least one of the ends. Optionally, specify a cross feature name after the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">end </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">keyword.</span></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="a8f1ef8d-158c-46e1-bfbc-63ba9ce12be9">Shorthand notation (available only for connector usages):<ol style="text-align: left;"><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare the <em style="text-align: left;">connection</em><em style="text-align: left;"> </em>keyword, specify the element name (optional) and</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">(optional)</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">. State the features explicitly (using the<span> </span></span><em style="text-align: left;">connect...to<span> </span></em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">syntax</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">)<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">or in a comma-separated list of related features prefixed with cross multiplicity. <br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5f3e2a47-0a2f-4b13-ad3e-b03881282ef5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>With shorthand notation, cross features in ends do not accept names; ends do not accept ordering. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="e4a0fe80-3731-4bf0-99f9-f5a022fc0429">Click the ac:macro-id="61d67a40-6283-4b55-a5d7-98e89cf9841f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* ------------ connection definition ------------ */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* cross features owned by the ends; Step 1.b */</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DeviceConnection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">crossHub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span </em>is mandatory</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed cross features in ends:</span></span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span OR (equivalent to the above)</span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkConnections</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ([</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">hub</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkHubs,</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.4</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">device</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">networkDevices);</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3 style="text-align: left;"><span style="letter-spacing: -0.006em;">Moving cross features between end types and ends</span></h3><p>Although there are two options for specifying cross features for ends, you can easily refactor connector definition ends from one option to the other. </p><p>To move cross features between end types and ends</p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="1fa72273-702f-43c9-8ea8-b848414e0114">Right-click the end of a connector definition and in the shortcut menu, select<span> </span><strong>Refactor</strong>, then<span> click one of the following:</span><ul><li><span><strong>Move Cross Feature to End</strong> to move cross features declared in the related types of the connector definition to the connector ends.</span></li><li><strong>Move Cross Feature to End Type </strong>to move cross features declared in the connector ends to <span>the related types of the connector definition.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="cross_features_refactoring.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ul></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577895 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00553: Custom creation dialogs

- page_id: `249577895`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577895/Custom+creation+dialogs
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-06-26T11:33:29.153+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model customization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are several creation dialogs and submenus available in the modeling tool:

- Element Creation dialog and/or submenu are available via the Create Element command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific elements.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.
- Relationship Creation dialog and/or submenu are available via the Create Relation command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific relations.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Relationship Creation dialog' space=''] page.
- View Creation dialog and/or submenu are available via the Create View command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific views.
  - See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom View Creation dialog' space=''] page.

The corresponding commands are available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element.

Instead of using predefined creation dialogs that come with the modeling tool, you can create custom model-based creation dialogs listing only the commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the creation dialogs based on the needs of your specific project.

600450

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Relationship Creation submenu, Element Creation dialog, and View Creation submenu.

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space='']*3DS SysML Customization* library contains the *DassaultSystemesUIComponents*namespace that enables the customization of creation dialogs:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- <DS_UIComponents> DassaultSystemesUIComponents
  - CoreUIComponents
    - **Operations**. Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.
    - **Components**. Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.
    - **Palette**. Contains concepts designed for view palette creation.
    - **Dialogs**. Contains concepts designed for Creation dialog customization.
  - **UIComponents**.
    - Contains a predefined view creation dialog.
    - **ViewsTemplates**. Contains model elements used to define the actions of templated buttons for creation dialogs, which in turn duplicate the template into the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are several creation dialogs and submenus available in the modeling tool: </p><ul><li data-uuid="514bd913-5253-459c-9dad-410c17d6eebe"><strong>Element Creation dialog and/or submenu </strong>are available via the <strong>Create Element </strong>command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific elements.<ul><li data-uuid="d47a98b8-70fd-4586-9995-f1366950938d">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom Element Creation dialog" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="8c34729e-22aa-4df4-99f8-4926961ed33c"><strong>Relationship Creation dialog and/or submenu</strong> are available via the <strong>Create Relation </strong>command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific relations.<ul><li data-uuid="566141d9-c3e9-4c86-8c30-53e74fef8f26">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom Relationship Creation dialog" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="29fd38fd-e802-46ff-8766-49846d429771"><strong>View Creation dialog and/or submenu</strong> are available via the <strong>Create View </strong>command. The dialog and submenu display a list of commands for creating specific views.<ul><li data-uuid="25e10244-95cf-466b-89b9-6f5d86c50fe6"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d2eef899-efd8-4aaa-8d75-7fb566db6982">See</ac:inline-comment-marker> the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom View Creation dialog" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li></ul><p>The corresponding commands are available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element. </p><p>Instead of using predefined creation dialogs that come with the modeling tool, you can create custom model-based creation dialogs listing only the commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the creation dialogs based on the needs of your specific project.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3dada395-e986-47da-ba02-6a34c265bfdb"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">600</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://youtu.be/1DV64W03MAc" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">450</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creation dialogs.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Relationship Creation submenu, Element Creation dialog, and View Creation submenu.</h6><h3>3DS SysML Customization library structure</h3><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:link-body><em>3DS SysML Customization</em> library</ac:link-body></ac:link> contains the <em>DassaultSystemesUIComponents </em>namespace that enables the customization of creation dialogs:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="230"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DS_SysML_Customization_library_structure.png" /></ac:image></p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="10b31599-d5d1-48f2-bfb7-bbcecfcdaf7e"><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">&lt;DS_UIComponents&gt; DassaultSystemesUIComponents</span></strong><ul><li><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">CoreUIComponents</span></strong><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Operations</strong>.<span> </span></span>Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Components</strong>.<span> </span></span>Contains concepts designed for view palette creation and Creation dialog customization.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Palette</strong>.<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contains concepts designed<span> </span></span>for view<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palette</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span> </span>creation.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Dialogs</strong>.<span> </span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contains concepts designed<span> </span></span>for Creation dialog customization.</li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>UIComponents</strong>.</span><ul><li>Contains a predefined view creation dialog.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>ViewsTemplates</strong>.<span> </span></span>Contains model elements used to define the actions of templated buttons for creation dialogs, which in turn duplicate the template into the model.</li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=280231950 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00554: Custom Element Creation dialog

- page_id: `280231950`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/280231950/Custom+Element+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2026-02-09T12:11:50.872+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model customization > Custom creation dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Element Creation dialog and/or submenuare available via the**Create Element**command.This command is available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element or its symbol in a view.The dialog and the submenu display a list of commands for creating specific elements. Additionally, all commands can be structured into named categories.

While there is a predefined submenu and dialog for displaying the predefined element creation commands, you can either customize the predefined dialog or create a new one from scratch. This way, you cancreate a custom model-based Element Creation dialog, listing only the element creation commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the Element Creation dialog based on the needs of your specific project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image on the left shows the Element Creation dialog according to the predefined*SysMLElementCreationDialog*configuration. The image on the right shows the custom Element Creation submenu.

##### Element Creation dialog composition and customization

The Create Element command can invoke either an Element Creation dialog or the command's submenu. The submenu is displayed when there are no more than ten element creation commands. Otherwise, the Element Creation dialog is displayed instead of the submenu. Both the dialog and the submenu display the command categories containing the element creation commands.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the Element Creation dialog (predefined) and the Element Creation submenu (custom) with commands structured into categories.

There are two options for customizing the Element Creation dialog/submenu:

- Customizing the predefined SysMLElementCreationDialog configuration for the Element Creation dialog/submenu. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.
- Creating the Element Creation dialog/submenu from scratch via the conceptual *ElementCreationDialog*definition. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.

The element dialog/submenu, command categories, and individual commands have specific attributes available for customization. Whether you choose to customize the predefined dialog that comes with the modeling tool or create a new one from scratch, the attributes remain the same. Additionally, if you choose to customize the predefined dialog, you can employ the *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents* package with the *SysMLElementCreationDialog* dialog definition, which contains predefined command categories and their commands. Reusing it allows you to easily modify the existing dialog, tailoring it to your needs.

You can activate the needed Element Creation dialog via the **Active Create Element Dialog**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE


## PAGE 00555: Custom Relationship Creation dialog

- page_id: `280756312`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/280756312/Custom+Relationship+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-01-02T12:06:21.772+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model customization > Custom creation dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Relationship Creation dialog and/or submenuare available via the**Create Relation**command.This command is available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element or its symbol in a view.The dialog and the submenu display a list of commands for creating specific relations between elements. Additionally, all commands can be structured into named categories, typically Incoming and Outgoing.

While there is a predefined submenu and dialog for displaying the predefined relationship creation commands, you can either customize the predefined dialog or create a new one from scratch. This way, you cancreate a custom model-based Relationship Creation dialog, listing only the relationship creation commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the Relationship Creation dialog based on the needs of your specific project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image on the left shows the Relationship Creation dialog according to the predefined*SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog*configuration. The image on the right shows the customized Relationship Creation submenu.

##### Relationship Creation dialog composition and customization

The Create Relation command can invoke either a Relationship Creation dialog or the command's submenu. The submenu is displayed when there are no more than ten relationship creation commands. Otherwise, the Relationship Creation dialog is displayed instead of the submenu. Both the dialog and the submenu display the command categories containing the relationship creation commands.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the Relationship Creation dialog (predefined) and the Relationship Creation submenu (custom) with commands structured into categories.

There are two options for customizing the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu:

- Customizing the predefined SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog configuration for the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Relationship Creation dialog' space=''] page.
- Creating the Relationship Creation dialog/submenu from scratch via the conceptual *RelationshipCreationDialog*definition. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Relationship Creation dialog' space=''] page.

The relationship dialog/submenu, command categories, and individual commands have specific attributes available for customization. Whether you choose to customize the predefined dialog that comes with the modeling tool or create a new one from scratch, the attributes remain the same. Additionally, if you choose to customize the predefined dialog, you can employ the *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents* package with the *SysMLRelationshipCreationDialog* dialog definition, which contains predefined command categories and their commands. Reusing it allows you to easily modify the existing dialog, tailoring it to your needs.

You can activate the needed Relationship Creation dialog via the **Active Relationship Element Dialog**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating Relationship Creation dialog' space=''] page.
