<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577850 space=SYSML2P version=38 -->
## PAGE 00556: Custom style sheets

- page_id: `249577850`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577850/Custom+style+sheets
- version_number: 38
- version_date: `2026-06-05T17:17:27.572+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Symbol styles
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules.

##### Custom style sheet types

Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. You can create style sheets in the following ways:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extracting style sheets' space=''] In a view displaying elements with applied metadata, you can automatically extract a style sheet based on the applied metadata, applied metadata feature value, specialized library element, or library element feature value. The extracted style sheet is automatically applied to the view and the displayed symbols.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheets' space=''] This option allows you to create a style sheet with your own custom rules.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheets' space=''] This way, you can create custom rules and customize the rules inherited from the reused style sheet.

##### Style sheet composition

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A style sheet is comprised of the following components:

- (Optional) Style sheet condition - specify a condition that applies to the entire style sheet. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the style sheet condition. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a style sheet condition' space=''] page.
- Rules - specify conditions and styles for symbols.
  - Condition - specifies to which symbols the rule applies. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] page.
  - Style - specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition. You must specify the style definition (e.g., Shape Style ) that comes with predefined attributes (e.g., fill color , font , etc.) available for specification. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] page.

#### INFO: Style definitions

Style definitions

There are several types of style definitions:

- **Primary style definitions** - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the *DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles* of the *3DS SysML Customization*library.
  - For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
- **Auxiliary style definitions** - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:
  - **Predefined style definitions** - style definitions specified in the *DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions* package of the *3DS SysML Customization*library (e.g., *AbstractGreenStyle*, *AbstractRedStyle*, etc.). These styles are only used in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined style sheets' space=''].
    - For a list of predefined style definitions, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
  - **Custom style definitions** - style definitions you create yourself. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating reusable styles' space=''] page.

##### Example

Say you prefer to use the *DefaultStyleSheet,* but you want to modify it so that part usage element symbols display all inherited elements at all times, which is disabled by default in the *DefaultStyleSheet.*

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating style sheets' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating rules for a style sheet' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] for the rule.
  1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying a condition for a rule' space=''] in the condition compartment by doing the following:
    1. In the Match By dropdown menu, select Element Kind .
    2. Specify the Metaclass field:
      1. In the Containment tab, click Quick Find , and input 'partusage'. In the results section, locate the Part Usage element with the metaclass symbol [ATTACHMENT filename='metaclass icon.png'] . If the element is not displayed, deselect the Apply Filter check box. The element is selected in the Containment tree. [ATTACHMENT filename='quick find.png']
      2. Drag and drop the element from the Containment tree onto the Metaclass field in the Specification panel. The metaclass has been specified as part usage. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying the condition for a style sheet rule.png']
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying styles for a rule' space=''] in the style attributes compartment by doing the following:
    1. In the Type dropdown menu, select Feature Compartment Style . If you take a look at the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Style sheet styles tables' space=''], you can see that the *showInherited*attribute is available via the *Feature Compartment Style*style definition. You need to specify it for the rule's condition to inherit the *showInherited*attribute*.*For a list of all style definitions, their attributes, value types, and symbols to which they apply, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Style sheet styles tables' space=''].
    2. In the Owned attributes section, hover over the redefined attributes and click the Delete [ATTACHMENT filename='delete_button.png'] button. Note: a newly created rule has the Shape Style style definition, which includes redefinitions for fillColor (with a randomly assigned color value) and penColor (with a comparatively darker color value) attributes. Delete the redefining attributes to retain the default style sheet's values for these attributes. [ATTACHMENT filename='removing owned attributes.png']
    3. In the Inherited attributes section, locate the showInherited attribute and click to edit it. Select the check box to set the attribute's value to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='modify inherited attribute value.png'] The style attributes compartment looks like this: [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying the style for a style sheet rule.png']
3. Implement the changes:
  1. (Optional) Now, the new style sheet is applied to the view it was created for. If you prefer to apply it to the entire project, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying style sheets' space=''] page.
  2. Refresh the project to implement the changes by doing one of the following:
    1. P ress the **F5**key on the keyboard.
    2. In the main toolbar, click **View**> **Refresh**.
  3. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''] . The showInherited attribute is one of the layout-affecting attributes, so you need to approve the style changes for affected symbols. See the procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Assessing layout-sensitive changes' space=''] page. [ATTACHMENT filename='Assessing layout-sensitive changes.png']
  4. The inherited elements can only be displayed in their appropriate compartments. If the compartment is suppressed, display it to see the inherited elements. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space=''] page. [ATTACHMENT filename='displaying compartments.png']

The *DefaultStyleSheet* remains applied to the entire project, while the specific view now additionally uses the new style sheet, affecting only part usage symbols; i.e., all of the styles defined in the *DefaultStyleSheet* remain, the only change is that now inherited elements are displayed in part usage symbols.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Instead of or in addition to using the default style sheet that specifies the default symbol styles, you can create your own custom model-based symbol styles <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">by creating style sheets with your own sets of rules. </span></p><h3>Custom style sheet types</h3><p>Custom model-based symbol styles are specified in a style sheet. You can create style sheets in the following ways:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extracting style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Extract a style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><br />In a view displaying elements with applied metadata, you can automatically extract a style sheet based on the applied metadata, applied metadata feature value, specialized library element, or library element feature value. All individual rules declared in the style sheet apply only to the symbols that comply with the style sheet condition. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a style sheet condition" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><strong>Rules </strong>- specify conditions and styles for symbols.<strong> </strong><ul><li><strong>Condition </strong>- specifies to which symbols the rule applies. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying a condition for a rule" /></ac:link> page.</li><li><strong>Style </strong>- specifies the style that will be applied to the symbols that comply with the specified condition. You must specify the style definition (e.g., <em>Shape Style</em>) that comes with predefined attributes (e.g., <em>fill color</em>, <em>font</em>, etc.) available for specification. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying styles for a rule" /></ac:link> page.</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="022a23a7-04fb-4c76-b543-22895607abe3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Style definitions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">There are several types of style definitions<span>:</span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Primary style definitions</strong> - high-level style definitions that specify style attributes that can be used for a rule. They are contained in the <em>DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles</em> of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library. </span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For a list of primary style definitions, see the 'Primary style definitions' column in the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Auxiliary style definitions</strong> - lower-level style definitions that reuse (subclassify) primary style definitions, specifying selected attributes and their values of the primary style definition. Auxiliary style definitions allow you to specify concrete styles which can then be reused in multiple rules instead of specifying the style for each rule locally. There are two types of auxiliary style definitions:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Predefined style definitions</strong> - style definitions specified in the <em>DS_Styles::StyleSheets::ColorDefinitions</em> package of the <em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library (e.g., <em>AbstractGreenStyle</em>, <em>AbstractRedStyle</em>, etc.). These styles are only used in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Predefined style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[predefined style sheets]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">For a list of predefined style definitions, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><strong>Custom style definitions</strong> - style definitions you create yourself. See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating reusable styles" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc67d3ee-731a-40f0-9f1d-03baca80e964"><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Example</h3><p>Say you prefer to use the <em>DefaultStyleSheet,</em> but you want to modify it so that part usage element symbols display all inherited elements at all times, which is disabled by default in the <em>DefaultStyleSheet.</em></p><ol><li data-uuid="83c52ca2-f58d-4f72-a2fc-701db60d4974"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating style sheets" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a new style sheet]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating rules for a style sheet" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[a rule for it]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em>.</em></li><li data-uuid="b9bcaa28-54e2-485d-bf5f-b908aec18341"><ac:link ac:anchor="Opening the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Open the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for the rule. <ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying a condition for a rule" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the condition for the rule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <strong>condition </strong>compartment by doing the following:<ol><li>In the <strong>Match By </strong>dropdown menu, select <em>Element Kind</em>.</li><li>Specify the <strong>Metaclass </strong>field:<ol><li>In the Containment tab, click <strong>Quick Find</strong>, and input 'partusage'. In the results section, locate the Part Usage element with the metaclass symbol <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="metaclass icon.png" /></ac:image>. If the element is not displayed, deselect the <strong>Apply Filter </strong>check box. The element is selected in the Containment tree. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick find.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Drag and drop the element from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Metaclass </strong>field in the Specification panel.<br />The metaclass has been specified as part usage.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying the condition for a style sheet rule.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying styles for a rule" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specify the style for the rule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in the <strong>style attributes </strong>compartment by doing the following:<ol><li>In the <strong>Type </strong>dropdown menu, select <em>Feature Compartment Style</em>.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6760f1a6-a477-4b50-a128-8cbe42df4a9e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you take a look at the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can see that the <em>showInherited </em>attribute is available via the <em>Feature Compartment Style </em>style definition. You need to specify it for the rule's condition to inherit the <em>showInherited </em>attribute<em>.</em></p><p>For a list of all style definitions, their attributes, value types, and symbols to which they apply, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Symbol styles table"><ri:page ri:content-title="Style sheet styles tables" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Symbol styles table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li data-uuid="6f125076-ce2e-4e5b-8b11-5a07175282ea">In the <strong>Owned </strong>attributes section, hover over the redefined attributes and click the <strong>Delete</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete_button.png" /></ac:image><strong> </strong>button. Note: a newly created rule has the <em>Shape Style </em>style definition, which includes redefinitions for <em>fillColor </em>(with a randomly assigned color value)<em> </em>and <em>penColor </em>(with a comparatively darker color value) attributes. Delete the redefining attributes to retain the default style sheet's values for these attributes.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing owned attributes.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="77deaa31-f0cd-4c80-a358-798d6107a06e">In the <strong>Inherited </strong>attributes section, locate the <em>showInherited </em>attribute and click to edit it. Select the check box to set the attribute's value to <em>true</em>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modify inherited attribute value.png" /></ac:image><br /> The <strong>style attributes </strong>compartment looks like this:<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying the style for a style sheet rule.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol></li><li data-uuid="a476d594-bfc9-44fa-afbc-ab8a705a4fe3">Implement the changes:<ol><li>(Optional) Now, the new style sheet is applied to the view it was created for. If you prefer to apply it to the entire project, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying style sheets" /></ac:link> page. </li><li>Refresh the project to implement the changes by doing one of the following:<ol><li>P<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ress the <strong>F5 </strong>key on the keyboard.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">In the main toolbar, click <strong>View </strong>&gt; <strong>Refresh</strong>. </span></li></ol></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assessing layout-sensitive changes" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Assess layout-sensitive changes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The <em>showInherited</em> attribute is one of the layout-affecting attributes, so you need to approve the style changes for affected symbols. See the procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assessing layout-sensitive changes" /></ac:link> page.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Assessing layout-sensitive changes.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="9577eed6-b1c9-497a-a95d-9cd13c9a9256">The inherited elements can only be displayed in their appropriate compartments. If the compartment is suppressed, display it to see the inherited elements. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying and suppressing compartments"><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Compartments]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="displaying compartments.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p>The <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em> remains applied to the entire project, while the specific view now additionally uses the new style sheet, affecting only part usage symbols; i.e., all of the styles defined in the <em>DefaultStyleSheet</em> remain, the only change is that now inherited elements are displayed in part usage symbols.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577731 space=SYSML2P version=9 -->
## PAGE 00557: Custom symbolic view definitions

- page_id: `249577731`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577731/Custom+symbolic+view+definitions
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-02-05T10:07:56.308+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Symbolic views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined symbolic view definitions, you can create model-based custom symbolic view definitions as part of your projects.By defining custom view definitions, you can model symbolic views with customized palettes.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']An example of a custom symbolic view definition *Requirements View*and a view *Stakeholder Needs*defined by the custom-created view definition*.*

##### Custom symbolic view definition composition

Custom symbolic view definitions differ in the view palettes they may have. A view palette contains button categories, which contain category-specific buttons. Button categories may also contain menu buttons with individual buttons in their submenus. The button categories also have specific attributes specifying the category name, tooltip, etc.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. 
The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.

Creating custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes is enabled by the , which contains all the necessary elements and configurations, allowing you to create your own symbolic view definitions with customized view palettes.

The library also contains the *Base Symbolic View*definition which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom symbolic view definitions. Itcarries the predefined view palette via the *baseViewPalette*part, containing Selection, Tools, and Common button categories. It also carries the rendering feature that indicates how the elements should be displayed in the view.See the .

##### General button categories table

The table below lists the buttons and attributes used in the general button categories - Selection, Tools, Common - used in all predefined symbolic view definitions. These button categories are also used in the *<bsv> 'Base Symbolic View'*via its *baseViewPalette.*You can find the button categories and predefined view palettes in *DS_Views::ViewPalettes*.

| Button categories | Category buttons | Category attributes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| SelectionCategory | selectionButtoncompleteCoverageModeButtonselectionMenu:selectSiblingButtonselectOfTheSameTypeButtonselectConnectedButtonselectConnectedRecursivelyButtonselectAllButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default false; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default false; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Selections"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Selection"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |
| ToolsCategory | stickyButtonpusherButtonmagnetButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default false; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default false; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Tools"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Tools"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |
| CommonCategory | textBoxMenu:textBoxButtontextBoxHtmlButtonseparatorMenu:horizontalSeparatorButtonverticalSeparatorButtonrectangularSeparatorButtondocButtoncommentButtonimageButton | attribute :>> isCollapsed default true; // attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by defaultattribute :>> hasVisibleLabels default true; // attribute specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or notattribute :>> label default "Common"; // attribute specifies the category nameattribute :>> description default "Common"; // attribute specifies the category tooltip |

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space='']*3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesViews*namespace that enables the custom symbolic view definition creation:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- **<DS_Views> DassaultSystemesViews**
  - **CoreViews**. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.
  - **SymbolicViews**. Contains predefined symbolic views.
  - **SymbolicViewsByExpression**. Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.
  - **TabularViews**. Contains predefined tabular views.
  - **ViewPalettes**. Contains predefined view palettes .
    - **CodeActionIdentifiers**. Contains action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view palettes . The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation.
    - **ElementTemplates**. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined symbolic view definitions, you can create model-based custom symbolic view definitions as part of your projects. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">By defining custom view definitions, you can model symbolic views with customized palettes.</span></span></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_diagram_kind.png" /></ac:image>An example of a custom symbolic view definition <em>Requirements View </em>and a view <em>Stakeholder Needs </em>defined by the custom-created view definition<em>.</em></h6><h3>Custom symbolic view definition composition</h3><p>Custom symbolic view definitions differ in the view palettes they may have. A view palette contains button categories, which contain category-specific buttons. Button categories may also contain menu buttons with individual buttons in their submenus. The button categories also have specific attributes specifying the category name, tooltip, etc. </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_palette_composition.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays the custom button category 'Requirements' that contains multiple category-specific buttons. 'requirement' (as well as 'software requirement' and 'constraint') is a menu button with submenu buttons. <br />The 'requirement' button is displayed as the default one for the menu button.   </h6><p><br /></p><p>Creating custom symbolic view definitions with customized palettes is enabled by the <ac:link ac:anchor="3DS SysML Customization library structure"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DS SysML Customization library]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, which contains all the necessary elements and configurations, allowing you to create your own symbolic view definitions with <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">customized view palettes.</span></span></p><p>The library also contains the <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Base Symbolic View</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> definition which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom symbolic view definitions. It </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">carries the predefined view palette via the <em>baseViewPalette </em>part, containing Selection, Tools, and Common button categories. It also carries the rendering feature that indicates how the elements should be displayed in the view. </span>See the <ac:link ac:anchor="General button categories table" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></p><h3>General button categories table</h3><p>The table below lists the buttons and attributes used in the general button categories - Selection, Tools, Common - used in all predefined symbolic view definitions. These button categories are also used in the <em>&lt;bsv&gt; 'Base Symbolic View' </em>via its <em>baseViewPalette. </em>You can find the button categories and predefined view palettes in <em>DS_Views::ViewPalettes</em>. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button categories</th><th>Category buttons</th><th>Category attributes</th></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">SelectionCategory</span></p></td><td><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectionButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">completeCoverageModeButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectionMenu:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectSiblingButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectOfTheSameTypeButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectConnectedButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectConnectedRecursivelyButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">selectAllButton</span></li></ul></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">hasVisibleLabels </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the labels of the contained buttons are displayed in the palette or not</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Selections&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the category name</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">description </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Selection&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the category tooltip</span></span></span></p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">ToolsCategory</span></p></td><td><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">stickyButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">pusherButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">magnetButton</span></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">hasVisibleLabels </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">false</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">labels of the contained buttons are</span></span> displayed in the palette or not</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Tools&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>name</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">description </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Tools&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>tooltip</span></span></span></p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">CommonCategory</span></p></td><td><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">textBoxMenu:</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">textBoxButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">textBoxHtmlButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">separatorMenu</span>:</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">horizontalSeparatorButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">verticalSeparatorButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">rectangularSeparatorButton</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">docButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">commentButton</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">imageButton</span></li></ul></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><ul><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">hasVisibleLabels </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies whether the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">labels of the contained buttons are</span></span> displayed in the palette or not</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Common&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">attribute </span></span>specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>name</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">description </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Common&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;   <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">category </span></span>tooltip</span></span></span></p></li></ul></div></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>3DS SysML Customization library structure</h3><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:link-body><em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library</ac:link-body></ac:link> contains the <em>DassaultSystemesViews </em>namespace that enables the custom symbolic view definition creation:</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DS_SysML_Customization_library_structure.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="6413891a-51cd-4da0-9dca-9a3db52924ae"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>&lt;DS_Views&gt; DassaultSystemesViews</strong></span><br /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CoreViews</strong>. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined symbolic views.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViewsByExpression</strong>. </span>Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>TabularViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined tabular views.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ViewPalettes</strong>. </span>Contains predefined view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palettes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CodeActionIdentifiers</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains </span>action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palettes</span>. The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation. </li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ElementTemplates</strong>. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304015636 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00558: Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu

- page_id: `304015636`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/304015636/Custom+table-specific+Add+New+button+dropdown+menu
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2026-05-06T18:57:40.590+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Tabular views > Managing tabular view rows
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a custom table-specific [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] to be used in the specific table's (tabular view usage or definition) **Add New**dropdown menu instead of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom Element Creation dialog' space=''] activated for the entire project. This way, you can control what elements are available in the **Add New** dropdown menu.

- This procedure also affects the suggested columns available for selection in the Configure Table Columns dialog. The elements you specify for the dropdown menu are also made available as the suggested columns in the Configure Table Columns dialog, including the meta-features of the specified metaclass/metadata.
- Keep in mind that if the specified elements do not satisfy the view's filters, they will not appear in the Add New dropdown menu, and the Add New button will be disabled.

###### Custom table-specific Add New button dropdown menu

To create a custom table-specific **Add New**button dropdown menu

1. Within the body of a tabular view, create a part usage that redefines the creationDialog and define it by the needed Element Creation Dialog configuration (e.g., DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog ).
2. Within the body of the part, create the needed Element Creation Dialog configuration. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page for relevant procedures. A custom table-specific Element Creation dialog is now used in the specific table's Add New dropdown menu.

view'Security Requirements':DS_Views::TabularViews::rt{ 
exposeSecurityStructure::*;

// redefines the inherited*creationDialog*and is defined by the conceptual Element Creation dialog*ElementCreationDialog*configuration: 
part:>>creationDialog:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::ElementCreationDialog{

// custom Element Creation Dialog configuration: 
partsysMLElementsMenu:>itemCategories{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Security"; 
part:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{ 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationByMetaclass:>>operation{ 
inref=SysML::Systems::RequirementUsage.metadata; // specifies a reuiqirement usage 
} 
} 
part:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Dialogs::DialogItem:>abstractItems{ 
attribute:>>label="security requirement"; 
performaction:DS_UIComponents::CoreUIComponents::Operations::OperationFromTemplate:>>operation{ 
inref= (SecurityRequirementTemplatemetaKerML::Package).ownedElement; // specifies custom element via its template 
} 
} 
} 
} 
} 
packageSecurityRequirementTemplate{ 
#SecurityStructure::Approvalrequirement; 
} 
packageSecurityStructure{ 
privateimportScalarValues::*; 
metadatadefApproval{ 
attributeapproved:Boolean; 
attributeapprover:String; 
} 
requirement<'1'>secureCommReq{ 
doc/* All external communication shall be encrypted using AES-256. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=true; 
:>>approver="Chief Security Engineer"; 
} 
} 
requirement<'2'>dataAtRestEncryptionReq{ 
doc/* All stored mission data shall be encrypted at rest using approved cryptographic algorithms. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=true; 
:>>approver="Data Protection Officer"; 
} 
} 
requirement<'3'>intrusionDetectionReq{ 
doc/* The drone shall detect and log repeated unauthorized access attempts. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=false; 
:>>approver="Cyber Defense Team"; 
} 
} 
requirement<'4'>emergencyOverrideReq{ 
doc/* An authenticated emergency override mechanism shall allow safe mission termination. */ 
@Approval{ 
:>>approved=true; 
:>>approver="Safety & Security Board"; 
} 
} 
}

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577761 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00559: Custom tabular view definitions

- page_id: `249577761`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577761/Custom+tabular+view+definitions
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2026-05-06T21:26:23.270+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Creating views > Tabular views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of, or in addition to, using the predefined tabular view definitions, you can create model-based custom tabular view definitions as part of your projects.By defining custom view definitions, you can model tabular views with customized columns.

##### Custom tabular view definition composition

Custom tabular view definitions differ in the columns they may have. While the tool comes with predefined tabular view definitions, you can model custom tabular view definitions with tailored columns.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of (1) predefined tabular view *Generic Table*, (2) customized *Generic Table* with the *Owned Element* columns removed, (3) custom *Drone Variant Table* with custom columns.

There are two ways you can create custom tabular view definitions:

- Reuse predefined tabular view definitions
  - Reusing a predefined tabular view definition allows the new view definition to inherit the columns defined for the reused definition. You can modify the inherited columns and add new custom ones, too.
- Create new customized tabular view definitions from scratch
  - A new view definition does not inherit any columns, allowing you to specify all the columns yourself.

Creating custom tabular view definitions with customized columns is enabled by the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Custom symbolic view definitions' space=''],which contains all the necessary elements and configurations allowing you to create your own tabular view definitions withcustomized columns.The library also contains the *Base Table*viewdefinition, which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom tabular view definitions. The library also carries predefined tabular view definitions with predefined columns.See the.

##### Predefined tabular views' columns table

The table below lists all the columns used in the predefined tabular view definitions.

| Standard predefined tabular view definition | Columns |
| --- | --- |
| Generic table | Declared NameDeclared Short NameDocumentationQualified NameOwnerOwned Element |
| Requirements table | Declared NameReq IDDocumentation |

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space='']*3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesViews*namespace that enables the custom tabular view definition creation:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- **<DS_Views> DassaultSystemesViews**
  - **CoreViews**. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.
  - **SymbolicViews**. Contains predefined symbolic views.
  - **SymbolicViewsByExpression**. Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.
  - **TabularViews**. Contains predefined tabular views.
  - **ViewPalettes**. Contains predefined view palettes .
    - **CodeActionIdentifiers**. Contains action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. They can also be used for custom view palettes . The predefined buttons contain names, descriptions, and actions for specific element creation.
    - **ElementTemplates**. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

While the tool comes with predefined tabular view definitions, you can model custom tabular view definitions with tailored columns.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="custom_tabular_views.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of (1) predefined tabular view <em>Generic Table</em>, (2) customized <em>Generic Table</em> with the <em>Owned Element</em> columns removed, (3) custom <em>Drone Variant Table</em> with custom columns.</h6><p>There are two ways you can create custom tabular view definitions:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bfc9336c-57f3-4df7-9920-ca2b3c0bd4f7">Reuse predefined tabular view definitions<ul><li data-uuid="800a6c17-038a-417c-a917-d10b205072ab">Reusing a predefined tabular view definition allows the new view definition to inherit the columns defined for the reused definition. You can modify the inherited columns and add new custom ones, too.</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="da02f915-87c5-4fa7-9c32-96d08eb8b5cb">Create new customized tabular view definitions from scratch<ul><li data-uuid="fa78723d-97a9-4c94-950f-d2d19f08d650">A new view definition does not inherit any columns, allowing you to specify all the columns yourself.  </li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: left;">Creating custom tabular view definitions with customized columns is enabled by the<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="3DS SysML Customization library structure"><ri:page ri:content-title="Custom symbolic view definitions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[3DS SysML Customization library]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,<span> </span>which contains all the necessary elements and configurations allowing you to create your own tabular view definitions with<span> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">customized columns. </span>The library also contains the <em>Base Table </em>view<span> definition, which is especially useful as it can be used as the basis for custom tabular view definitions. The library also carries predefined tabular view definitions with predefined columns</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.<span> </span></span>See the<span> </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Predefined tabular views' columns table" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></p><h3>Predefined tabular views' columns table</h3><p>The table below lists all the columns used in the predefined tabular view definitions.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Standard predefined tabular view definition</th><th scope="col">Columns</th></tr><tr><td>Generic table</td><td><ul><li data-uuid="dfc97d9e-9a32-4610-86e6-70a372d525cc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declared Name</span></li><li data-uuid="8b889249-db77-4a77-ad77-64bf09ef6ec8"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declared Short Name</span></li><li data-uuid="ebd5ee9d-b0e4-40e6-8a6c-fe3c6b45f85e"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Documentation</span></li><li data-uuid="6587d964-b5cc-466f-87cb-cab2c7ecc8d0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Qualified Name</span></li><li data-uuid="fac921b0-ca4c-4916-9d3e-7bf25d29b2c4"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Owner</span></li><li data-uuid="5adf43f4-5a5a-43a1-9a3b-c8647c0f2c5c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Owned Element</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>Requirements table</td><td><ul style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="790250fd-d3d8-46a4-a465-c24c4d972637"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declared Name</span></li><li data-uuid="b56dc1e5-0087-474a-993d-5a7b180b26cd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Req ID</span></li><li data-uuid="bb3ed24d-d2a2-4c71-8dc4-3fedbb0e2712"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Documentation</span></li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>3DS SysML Customization library structure</h3><p style="text-align: left;">The<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:link-body><em>3DS SysML Customization<span> </span></em>library</ac:link-body></ac:link> contains the <em style="text-align: left;">DassaultSystemesViews </em>namespace that enables the custom tabular view definition creation:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DS_SysML_Customization_library_structure.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="0720473d-8195-4f47-bcbf-b99f0ae408b1"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>&lt;DS_Views&gt; DassaultSystemesViews</strong></span><br /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CoreViews</strong>. Contains abstract view definitions for symbolic and tabular view definition creation; tabular view column and cell modeling concepts, their rendering definitions and usages; style sheet activation concepts.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined symbolic views.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>SymbolicViewsByExpression</strong>. </span>Contains predefined symbolic views by expression.</li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>TabularViews</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains predefined tabular views.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>ViewPalettes</strong>. </span>Contains predefined view <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">palettes</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">.</span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CodeActionIdentifiers</strong>. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Contains </span>action IDs for the predefined buttons used in the symbolic view palettes of all predefined symbolic view definitions. Contains certain element templates for view palettes.</span></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=286557635 space=SYSML2P version=14 -->
## PAGE 00560: Custom validation suites and rules

- page_id: `286557635`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/286557635/Custom+validation+suites+and+rules
- version_number: 14
- version_date: `2026-02-13T18:07:29.645+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Instead of or in addition to using the predefined validation suites and their rules, you can create your own model-based validation suites with custom validation rules.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Validation suites composition

Validation suites are created as part definitions that contain validation rules created as part usages. The validation suite is only comprised of validation rules.

##### Custom validation rule's composition

A custom validation rule is comprised of the following components:

- Name . The name of the failed validation rule.
- Severity. The failed validation rule's severitylevel .
- Message . The failed validation rule's message explaining the failure.
- Condition . The condition expression specifying the validation rule. If the condition expression evaluates to false for a certain element, the element fails the validation.

For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating validation suites and rules' space=''] page.

##### 3DS SysML Customization library structure

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Libraries & samples' space='']*3DS SysML Customization*library contains the *DassaultSystemesValidation* namespace, dedicated to validation suites and rules customization:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- **<DS_Validation>DassaultSystemesValidation**
  - **CoreValidationComponents.**Contains concepts for creating custom validation suites and validation rules.****
  - **Suites.**Contains predefined validation suite instances.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Instead of or in addition to using the predefined validation suites and their rules, you can create your own model-based validation suites with custom validation rules. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Custom Validation Suites and Rules.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Validation suites composition</h3><p>Validation suites are created as part definitions that contain validation rules created as part usages. The validation suite is only comprised of validation rules.  </p><h3>Custom validation rule's composition</h3><p>A custom validation rule is comprised of the following components:</p><ul><li data-uuid="323b6f81-cba2-4d42-958c-c78678a7313d"><strong>Name</strong>. The name of the failed validation rule.</li><li data-uuid="f1333748-0cec-4b48-a063-335adb5345f9"><strong>Severity. </strong>The failed validation rule's <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/286557687/Reviewing+validation+results#Reviewingvalidationresults-Severitylevel">severity<span> </span>level</a><span>. </span></li><li data-uuid="7ecf021e-79ba-4f65-b8a0-7774f6ab8a6c"><strong>Message</strong>. The failed validation rule's message explaining the failure.</li><li data-uuid="0c9abbd8-b7a9-4511-92e0-e48849de5089"><strong>Condition</strong>. The condition expression specifying the validation rule. If the condition expression evaluates to false for a certain element, the element fails the validation. </li></ul><p>For specific procedures, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating validation suites and rules" /></ac:link> page.</p><h3>3DS SysML Customization library structure</h3><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Libraries &amp; samples" /><ac:link-body><em>3DS SysML Customization </em>library</ac:link-body></ac:link> contains the <em>DassaultSystemesValidation</em> namespace, dedicated to validation suites and rules customization:</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3DS_SysML_Customization_library_structure.png" /></ac:image></p><ul><li data-uuid="6f436f4c-4098-4458-96e5-0d791caf190a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>&lt;DS_Validation&gt;DassaultSystemesValidation</strong></span><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>CoreValidationComponents. </strong>Contains concepts for creating custom validation suites and validation rules.<strong><br /></strong></span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><strong>Suites. </strong>Contains predefined validation suite instances.</span></li></ul></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577897 space=SYSML2P version=8 -->
## PAGE 00561: Custom View Creation dialog

- page_id: `249577897`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577897/Custom+View+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 8
- version_date: `2026-02-05T15:10:30.486+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model customization > Custom creation dialogs
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The View Creation dialog and/or submenuare available via the**Create View**command.This command is available in the element shortcut menu displayed upon right-clicking an element or its symbol in a view.The dialog and the submenu display a list of commands for creating specific views. Additionally, all commands can be structured into named categories.

While there is a predefined submenu and dialog for displaying the predefined view creation commands, you can either customize the predefined dialog or create a new one from scratch. This way, you cancreate a custom model-based View Creation dialog, listing only the view creation commands of your choice, allowing you to customize the View Creation dialog based on the needs of your specific project.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The image on the left shows the View Creation submenu according to the predefined*SysMLViewCreationDialog*configuration. The image on the right shows the customized View Creation submenu.

##### View Creation dialog composition and customization

The Create View command can invoke either a View Creation dialog or the command's submenu. The submenu is displayed when there are no more than ten view creation commands. Otherwise, the View Creation dialog is displayed instead of the submenu. Both the dialog and the submenu display the command categories containing the view creation commands.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The image displays the View Creation dialog (custom) and the View Creation submenu (predefined) with commands structured into categories.

There are two options for customizing the View Creation dialog/submenu:

- Customizing the predefined SysMLViewCreationDialog configuration for the View Creation dialog/submenu. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing View Creation dialog' space=''] page.
- Creating the View Creation dialog/submenu from scratch via the conceptual *ViewCreationDialog*definition. For specific procedures, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating View Creation dialog' space=''] page.

The view dialog/submenu, command categories, and individual commands have specific attributes available for customization. Whether you choose to customize the predefined dialog that comes with the modeling tool or create a new one from scratch, the attributes remain the same. Additionally, if you choose to customize the predefined dialog, you can employ the *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents* package with the *SysMLViewCreationDialog* dialog definition, which contains predefined command categories and their commands. Reusing it allows you to easily modify the existing dialog, tailoring it to your needs.

You can activate the needed View Creation dialog via the **Active Create View Dialog**. See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating View Creation dialog' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=280231953 space=SYSML2P version=19 -->
## PAGE 00562: Customizing Element Creation dialog

- page_id: `280231953`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/280231953/Customizing+Element+Creation+dialog
- version_number: 19
- version_date: `2026-02-09T12:35:08.436+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model customization > Custom creation dialogs > Custom Element Creation dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

See the following procedures for customizing the predefined *SysMLElementCreationDialog* configuration for the Element Creation dialog and submenu.

#### NOTE: Updating the Element Creation dialog/submenu

Updating the Element Creation dialog/submenu

Once you have created the new Element Creation dialog/submenu, activate it via the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activating Element Creation dialog' space=''].

The procedures on this page use the example of customizing the*SysMLElementCreationDialog***part definition. However, you can similarly customize any other custom element creation dialog customization part definition you may have.

##### Reusing an Element Creation dialog/submenu

To reuse an Element Creation dialog/submenu

1. In the Containment tree, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing elements' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Textual Editor' space=''] for the new namespace.
3. In the Textual Editor, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space=''] and name it.
4. Within the body of the package, declare a part definition, specify the name for the dialog instance, and subclassify the SysMLElementCreationDialog definition from the DS_UIComponents::UIComponents package, then, within the body of the definition:
  1. 
  2. 
  3. 
5. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomizedElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog{// redefining and renaming the *SysMLElementCreationDialog* 
} 
}

##### Renaming the Create Element command

The default name for the Element Creation dialog's invocation command is **Create Element**.

To rename the Create Element command

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new Element Creation dialog instance, redefine the actionName attribute with the new element creation command name.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomizedElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog{ 
attribute:>>actionNamedefault"Create SysMLv2 Element";// renaming the element creation command to "Create SysMLv2 Element" 
} 
}

##### Modifying the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

#### INFO: Command menus

Command menus

Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories predefined in the *3DS SysML Customization* library's *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog*are:

- sysMLElementsMenu - a singular element creation commands' category in the Element Creation dialog/submenu.

###### Creating element creation command categories for the Element Creation dialog/submenu

To create element creation command categories for the Element Creation dialog/submenu

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.

###### Removing element creation command categories from the Element Creation dialog/submenu

To remove element creation command categories from the Element Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new Element Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the specified definition (e.g., SysMLElementCreationDialog ), and set the multiplicity to zero.
2. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{partdefCustomizedElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLElementsMenu[0];// removing the sysMLElementsMenu command category by setting the multiplicity to zero 
} 
}

###### Modifying the element creation command category attributes of the Element Creation dialog/submenu

#### INFO: Command menu attributes

Command menu attributes

Element Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined for *sysMLElementsMenu*in *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog*are:

- attribute :>> label default "General" ; // attribute specifies the category name
- attribute:>> isCollapseddefaultfalse;// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default

If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten element creation commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the *isCollapsed* attribute is ignored.

To modify the element creation command category attributes of the Element Creation dialog/submenu

1. Within the body of the part definition element of the new Element Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the SysMLElementCreationDialog definition.
2. Within the body of the redefining category, redefine the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomizedElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLElementsMenu{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"SysMLv2 Elements";// naming the element creation command category 
attribute:>>isCollapseddefaulttrue;// modified category's default collapse state 
} 
} 
}

##### Modifying the Element Creation dialog/submenu commands

#### INFO: Commands in menus

Commands in menus

You can see all the Element Creation dialog/submenu commands predefined for the *sysMLElementsMenu* in *DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog::sysMLElementsMenu.*

###### Removing element creation commands from the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To remove element creation commands from the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining an Element Creation dialog/submenu command category*.*
2. Declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to remove and set its multiplicity to zero.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomizedElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLElementsMenu{ 
part:>>actionUsageItem[0];// removing the actionUsageItem element creation command from the sysMLElementsMenu category by setting the multiplicity to zero 
} 
} 
}

###### Renaming element creation commands in the Element Creation dialog/submenu command category

To rename element creation commands in the Element Creation dialog/submenu command category

1. Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining an Element Creation dialog/submenu command category and declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to modify.
2. Within the body of the redefining usage, redefine the inherited *label*attribute with the needed value.
3. Click the Synchronize button.

packageCustomElementCreationDialogs{ 
partdefCustomizedElementCreationDialog:>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLElementCreationDialog{ 
part:>>sysMLElementsMenu{ 
part:>>actionUsageItem{ 
attribute:>>labeldefault"Action Usage";// modified command name 
} 
} 
} 
}

###### Adding element creation commands to the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

To add element creation commands to the Element Creation dialog/submenu command categories

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] procedure on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Element Creation dialog' space=''] page.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
