style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span> :&gt; </u></span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> { <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// redefining and renaming the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog<br /></em></span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">   }<br />}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Renaming the Create View command</span></h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dc4e467-89a8-453a-9988-427ffa5f8437"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The default name for the View Creation dialog's invocation command is <strong>Create View</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To rename the Create View command</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="a065b4fc-0d54-4d5e-af1f-56fcebf5b4b4">Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the <em>actionName</em> attribute with the new view creation command name. </li><li data-uuid="e2109ce4-57f1-45d9-aeb3-e369c7253589"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="73c043ce-2665-49c5-8629-b999684bf9a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">actionName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;Create Diagram&quot;</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// renaming the view creation command to &quot;Create Diagram&quot;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9a77c693-3e4a-4ed4-a7d8-2e8270585d2f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command categories</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu command categories predefined in the <em>3DS SysML Customization</em> library's <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are: </p><ul><li data-uuid="0d208ba7-0503-4674-9e7a-f941f0d9e53a"><em>sysMLViewsMenu</em> - for the symbolic views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu. </li><li data-uuid="2c468e4d-621a-4523-82cc-30a56ae980eb"><em>sysMLTableMenu</em> - for the tabular views command category in the View Creation dialog/submenu.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span class="toc-item-body">Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu</span></h4><p><span class="toc-item-body">To create view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu</span></p><hr /><ul><li data-uuid="2b6197c0-a501-4120-b0e9-a47e7ac4181f"><span class="toc-item-body">See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating View Creation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating view creation command categories for the View Creation dialog/submenu]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> procedure on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating View Creation dialog" /></ac:link> page.</span></li></ul><h4>Removing view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><p><span>To remove view creation command categories from the View Creation dialog/submenu</span></p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="aa365c65-95bb-460a-bc49-0bbf0c9d0ef1">Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> definition and set the multiplicity to zero.</li><li data-uuid="55917ace-71f1-4b6d-a198-f43153652f09"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e8e66d6-7698-45ad-98f5-c6739db3895f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLTableMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>]</u>; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// removing the sysMLTableMenu command category by setting the multiplicity to zero</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Modifying the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7e30ba43-c8da-497e-9daf-45a436f217ce"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Command category attributes</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu command category attributes predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2725aea4-a54a-4e36-9039-d8ab57ed21d1"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="f39f2d1d-3b98-49cf-bc24-19055cfd44b0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3151637d-9b29-4ffe-ad9d-300e647f5daf">default</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Symbolic Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the Symbolic Views category name (via <em>sysMLViewsMenu</em>)<br /></span></li><li data-uuid="c4c53b11-dda8-4045-bc57-99cbf6babb56"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; label </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">&quot;<u>Tabular Views</u>&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// attribute specifies the Tabular Views category name (via <em>sysMLTableMenu</em>)</span></li><li data-uuid="ea7dc03a-c3ab-4a86-8967-18d9bd0167ca"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> :&gt;&gt; isCollapsed </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">true</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">; </span>// attribute specifies whether the category is collapsed or expanded by default</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c1f372b4-2387-4654-8e9e-76748d2ef534"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If the dialog/submenu contains fewer than ten view commands, then the category cannot be collapsed. In this case, the <em>isCollapsed</em> attribute is ignored.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To modify the view creation command category attributes of the View Creation dialog/submenu</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="c1a3070f-5c6b-4aa3-a030-defdf6a6ef6a">Within the body of the part definition element of the new View Creation dialog instance, redefine the command category you want to customize, inherited from the <em>SysMLViewCreationDialog</em> definition.</li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the redefining category, </span>redefine <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">the inherited attributes that you want to modify with the needed values.</span></li><li data-uuid="9d2bc424-07c8-4401-be3f-5e49c3e3c4df"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d85b0fb-cb16-4ef0-8d6a-b5ead1b9832f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206A83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206A83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206A83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// modified category's default collapse state</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#AE2E24);">&quot;SysML v2 Symbolic Views&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// naming the symbolic views' creation command category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">sysMLTableMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">isCollapsed</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">true</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// modified category's default collapse state</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">attribute</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326C);">label</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055CC);">default</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#AE2E24);">&quot;SysML v2 Tabular Views&quot;</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164B35);">// naming the tabular views' creation command category</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Modifying the View Creation dialog/submenu commands</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="465518da-8e26-400a-b5b8-7fd47b90ee9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Commands in categories</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>View Creation dialog/submenu commands predefined for <em>sysMLViewsMenu </em>and <em>sysMLTableMenu </em>in <em>DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog </em>are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5e50ad2a-7b75-49d7-87e7-a3e3ee5b4270"><em>sysMLViewsMenu:</em><ul><li><em>gvItem - </em>command for General View</li><li><em>ivItem - </em>command for Interconnection View</li><li><em>afvItem - </em>command for Action Flow View</li><li><em>stvItem - </em>command for State Transition View</li></ul></li><li data-uuid="92850224-2ef2-4e11-9dd0-f5773caba018"><em>sysMLTableMenu:</em><ul><li><em>gtItem </em>-<em> </em>command for General Table</li><li><em>rtItem</em> - command for Requirements Table</li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Removing view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To remove view creation commands from the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol style="text-align: left;"><li data-uuid="d4a14b66-8231-41cb-b0fc-b49767269475"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category<em>.</em></span></li><li data-uuid="9b0b04eb-1607-45d9-b0d8-c2f7ab6e65dd"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to remove and set its multiplicity to zero.</span></li><li data-uuid="345dca75-a7b2-46a0-aa5c-483afdd5a55a"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48143ee6-7510-4b2c-acf2-ca062bd903c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomizedViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">DS_UIComponents::UIComponents::SysMLViewCreationDialog</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">sysMLViewsMenu</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">            </span><u><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> :&gt;&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">afvItem</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span></u><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"><u>]</u>; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// removing the afvItem command from the sysMLViewsMenu category by setting the multiplicity to zero</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Renaming view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</h4><p>To rename view creation commands in the View Creation dialog/submenu command categories</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="32b106af-1ebb-4823-85f4-89d12d6c6f99"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Place the cursor within the body of the part element redefining a View Creation dialog/submenu command category </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">and declare a part usage, then redefine the command you want to modify.</span></li><li data-uuid="6c874d68-3f66-48d5-958a-e93fd4f1eba0"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Within the body of the redefining usage, redefine the inherited <em>label </em>attribute with the needed value.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><br /></span></li><li data-uuid="be761cfb-aa2f-4020-a7d8-24c381848ffc"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Click the<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">Synchronize<span> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">button.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="657cc2ff-7dc8-4a04-8146-1f8a6bb0c486"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">package</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">CustomViewCreationDialogs</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=314181949 space=SYSML2P version=1 -->
## PAGE 00566: Dassault Systemes Composition Integrity Validation Suite

- page_id: `314181949`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/314181949/Dassault+Systemes+Composition+Integrity+Validation+Suite
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-06-26T14:05:38.262+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis > Validation > Predefined validation suites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists all the validation rules in the *DassaultSystemesCompositionIntegrityValidationSuite*, along with their messages and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing validation results' space=''].

References such as *$notVariantUsages* are used in messages to indicate elements referenced by the validation rule. In the tool, they are replaced with hyperlinks to the actual elements requiring your attention to resolve the validation failure.

| Validation rule | Validation rule message | Severity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| validateRelationshipIsComplete | Must have a specified target.ConditionThe target is empty. | Error |
| Must have a specified source.ConditionThe source is empty. |  |  |
| Must have both a specified source and a target.ConditionBoth the target and the source are empty. |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

In the tool, they are replaced with hyperlinks to the actual elements requiring your attention to resolve the validation failure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Validation rule</th><th scope="col">Validation rule message</th><th scope="col">Severity</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><p>validateRelationshipIsComplete </p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Must have a specified target.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf8bddbd-0f36-44d1-97f7-da30c22deb7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The target is empty.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td rowspan="3">Error</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Must have a specified source.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5503934f-f8b9-4faf-851b-340f0244f315"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The s<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172B4D);">ource is empty.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Must have both a specified source and a target.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba76cf84-92b4-4f19-9393-f76b6aa56002"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Condition</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Both the target and the source are empty.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>

## PAGE 00567: Dassault Systemes Functions

- page_id: `296124506`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/296124506/Dassault+Systemes+Functions
- version_number: 9
- version_date: `2026-04-13T11:10:49.414+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis > Functions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists the functions available in the *DassaultSystemesFunctions* namespace of the 3DS SysML Customization library.

| Function | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| calc def IsEssentialElement { in input : Element; return [1] : Boolean; rep script language "elementFilter" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.model.EssentialElements::isEssentialInOwnershipContext*/} | filter DS_Functions::IsEssentialElement(self);Click here to see the full example...package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs; part vehicleRecord;}view 'Vehicle DetailsView' : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { expose 'Vehicle Information'::**; filter DS_Functions::IsEssentialElement(self); } | This function checks whether the <input> element is treated as an essential element.Can be used only in a . |
| calc def IsNonStandardLibraryElement { in input : Element; return [1] : Boolean; rep script language "elementFilter" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.kerml.libraries.StandardLibraries::isNotFromLibrary*/} | filter DS_Functions::IsNonStandardLibraryElement(self);Click here to see the full example...package 'Vehicle Information' { part vehicleSpecs; part vehicleRecord;}view 'Vehicle DetailsView' : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { expose 'Vehicle Information'::**; filter DS_Functions::IsNonStandardLibraryElement(self);} | This function checks whether the <input> element comes from a non-standard library.Can be used only in a . |
| calc def IsSpecific { in specific : Element; in general : Element; return [1] : Boolean; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.isSpecific */} | part def ModelLibraryElement :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { private import DS_Styles::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part electrical :> rule { part :>> condition : FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { IsSpecific(element, ElectricalPart.metadata) } } part :>> style : ShapeStyle { attribute :>> penColor default "#0078FF"; } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { part def ElectricalPart; part def MechanicalPart; part electrical : ElectricalPart; part mechanical : MechanicalPart;}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part system { part componentA :> electrical; part componentB :> mechanical; } view userModel : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { part : ModelLibraryElement :> explicitlyAppliedStyleSheets; }}part def ModelLibraryElement :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { part electrical :> rule { part :>> condition : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { DS_Functions::IsSpecific(element, 'Concepts & Keywords'::ElectricalPart.metadata) } } part :>> style : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle { attribute :>> penColor default "#0078FF"; } }} | This function checks whether the <specific> feature is directly or indirectly specialized by the <general> feature. |
| calc def GetAppliedMetadata { in element : Element; return [0..*] : Metadata::MetadataItem; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.getAppliedMetadata */} | part def 'ComponentImportance::isImportant' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { private import DS_Styles::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { (GetAppliedMetadata(element) as ComponentImportance).isImportant == true } } view :>> style : ShapeStyle { attribute :>> fillColor = "#CCAAAA"; } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { metadata def ComponentImportance { :>> annotatedElement : SysML::Systems::PartUsage; attribute isImportant : ScalarValues::Boolean; }}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part system { part componentA { @ComponentImportance { :>> isImportant = true; } } part componentB { @ComponentImportance { :>> isImportant = false; } } } view userModel : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { part : 'ComponentImportance::isImportant' :> explicitlyAppliedStyleSheets; }}part def 'ComponentImportance::isImportant' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { (DS_Functions::GetAppliedMetadata(element) as 'Concepts & Keywords'::ComponentImportance).isImportant == true } } view :>> style : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle { attribute :>> fillColor = "#CCAAAA"; } }} | This function returns all metadata usages that annotate the <input> element. |
| calc def GetSpecifiedBy { in specific [0..*] : Element; in general : Element; return [0..*] : Type = specific.? { in x; IsSpecific(x, general) };} | view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { private import DS_Views::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; expose UserModel::vehicle::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Color Attributes' : ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : ElementCellRendering :>> asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = GetSpecifiedBy((rowElement as SysML::Element).ownedElement, Color.metadata); } } } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { attribute def Color; attribute def Mass;}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part vehicle { attribute color : Color; attribute mass : Mass; }}view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { expose UserModel::vehicle::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Color Attributes' : DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : DS_Views::CoreViews::ElementCellRendering :>> DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = DS_Functions::GetSpecifiedBy((rowElement as SysML::Element).ownedElement, 'Concepts & Keywords'::Color.metadata); } } } }} | This function returns the <specific> feature if it is directly or indirectly specialized by the <general> feature. |
| calc def GetValue { in contextType : Element; in feature : Feature; return [0..*] : Base::Anything; language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.getValue */} | part def 'Electrical::isPowered' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { private import DS_Styles::**; private import DS_Functions::**; private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { GetValue(element, isPowered.metadata) == true } } part :>> style : ShapeStyle { attribute :>> lineWidth default 3; } }}Click here to see the full example...package 'Concepts & Keywords' { part def ElectricalPart; part electrical : ElectricalPart { attribute isPowered : ScalarValues::Boolean; } metadata def Electrical :> Metaobjects::SemanticMetadata { :>> baseType = electrical meta SysML::Systems::Usage; :>> annotatedElement : SysML::Systems::PartUsage; }}package UserModel { private import 'Concepts & Keywords'::**; part system { #Electrical part componentA { :>> isPowered = true; } #Electrical part componentB { :>> isPowered = false; } } view UserModel : DS_Views::SymbolicViews::gv { part : 'Electrical::isPowered' :> explicitlyAppliedStyleSheets; }}part def 'Electrical::isPowered' :> DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::StyleSheet { part 'true' :> rule { part :>> condition : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::Predicates::FreeFormCondition { calc :>> test { DS_Functions::GetValue(element, 'Concepts & Keywords'::electrical::isPowered.metadata) == true } } part :>> style : DS_Styles::CoreStylesComponents::KerMLStyles::ShapeStyle { attribute :>> lineWidth default 3; } }} | This function returns the values of all features in the <contextType> that match or redefine the specified <feature>. |
| calc def GetSuppliersOfOwnedDependency { in context : Element; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.getSuppliersOfOwnedDependency */ return [0..*] : Element;} | view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { private import DS_Views::**; private import DS_Functions::**; expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Supplier of Owned Dependency' : ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : ElementCellRendering :>> asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = GetSuppliersOfOwnedDependency(rowElement); } } } }}Click here to see the full example...package UserModel { part system { dependency from componentA to componentB; part componentA; part componentB; }}view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Supplier of Owned Dependency' : DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : DS_Views::CoreViews::ElementCellRendering :>> DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = DS_Functions::GetSuppliersOfOwnedDependency(rowElement); } } } }} | This function returns the list of suppliers from Dependency relations owned by the <context>, where <context> acts as the client of the dependency. |
| calc def IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency { in context : Element; in element : Element; rep script language "JavaMethod" /* com.dassault_systemes.modeler.sysml.ds_functions.DSFunctions.isNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency */ return [1] : Boolean;} | view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { private import DS_Views::**; private import DS_Functions::**; expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Not A Supplier of Owned Dependency' : ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : BooleanCellRendering :>> asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency(rowElement, componentB.metadata); } } } }}Click here to see the full example...package UserModel { part system { dependency from componentA to componentB; part componentA; part componentB; }}view userModel : DS_Views::TabularViews::gt { expose UserModel::system::**; filter not KerML::Root::Element::isLibraryElement; render rendering asTable :>> asTable { view 'Not A Supplier of Owned Dependency' : DS_Views::CoreViews::ColumnByExpressionView :> column { render rendering : DS_Views::CoreViews::BooleanCellRendering :>> DS_Views::CoreViews::asTableCell { calc :>> getValue { in rowElement; return result = DS_Functions::IsNotSupplierOfOwnedDependency(rowElement, componentB.metadata); } } } }} | This function checks whether the <element> is not among the suppliers of Dependency relations owned by the <context>, where <context> acts as the client of the dependency. |

acts as the client of the dependency.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=289374226 space=SYSML2P version=12 -->
## PAGE 00568: Dassault Systemes Validation Suite

- page_id: `289374226`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/289374226/Dassault+Systemes+Validation+Suite
- version_number: 12
- version_date: `2026-07-13T16:24:02.535+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis > Validation > Predefined validation suites
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists all the validation rules in the *DassaultSystemesValidationSuite*, along with their messages and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reviewing validation results' space=''].

References such as *$notVariantUsages* are used in messages to indicate elements referenced by the validation rule. In the tool, they are replaced with hyperlinks to the actual elements requiring your attention to resolve the validation failure.

| Validation rule | Validation rule message | Severity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| validateEnumType | Must be typed by exactly one enumeration definition. Currently typed by: $types. | Error |
| Must be typed by an enumeration definition. |  |  |
| validateUsageTypedByClassifier | Must be typed by a classifier. Currently typed by $featureTypingType. | Error |
| validateFeatureTypeConformance | Feature type(s) must conform to the type(s) of its value's result. The feature is typed by: $featureTypes. Non-conforming feature value result type(s): $nonConformingResultTypes. | Warning |
| validateShapeOwner | Shape ownership in the view must correspond to element ownership in the model.ConditionApplies to all OwningMemberships, except FeatureMembership. | Warning |
| Shape ownership in the view must correspond to element ownership or inheritance in the model.ConditionApplies to all FeatureMemberships. | Warning |  |
| validatePathOverlapping | A path must not overlap another path. | Warning |
| validateFlowEndDirectedFeatureCompatibility | The source output redefined feature $sourceOutput must have an 'out' or 'inout' direction.ConditionThe source referenced feature has an 'out' direction feature. | Error |
| The target input redefined feature $targetInput must have an 'in' or 'inout' direction.ConditionThe target referenced feature has an 'in' direction feature. |  |  |
| validateNamespaceDistinguishibility | The name $elementName must be distinguishable from other namespace members. Currently indistinguishable from: $element.ConditionWhen an owned element is marked as failed. | Error |
| Namespace members $elementName must be distinguishable from one another. Current indistinguishable members: $members.ConditionWhen an inherited element is marked as failed. |  |  |
| validateRootNamespaceMemberDistinguishibility | The name $elementName must be distinguishable from the names of top-level members of other root namespaces. Currently indistinguishable from: $element. | Error |
| validateExpressionSingleResultDeclarationvalidateFunctionSingleResultDeclaration | A result should be specified either by a feature value or by a result expression. Current result with feature value: $result. Current result expression: $resultExpression. | Warning |
| validateLowerBoundNotExceedsUpper | The lower bound of a multiplicity must be less than or equal to its upper bound. | Error |
| validateRedefinedInheritedFeatureShape | A feature redefined in the model must not be displayed as inherited in the view. | Warning |
| validateReferenceSubsettingTypeConformance | Feature type(s) must conform to the referenced feature type(s). The referenced feature $referencedFeature is typed by: $referencedFeatureTypes. Non-conforming type(s): $nonConformingFeatureTypes. | Error |
| validateTypeNoDuplicateFeatureRedefinition | A type must not have multiple features redefining the same feature. Current duplicate redefinitions: $duplicateRedefiningFeature redefine $redefinedFeature. | Error |
| validatePathRelatedElementCorrectness | Relation path's target in the view must correspond to its target in the model.ConditionIncorrect target. | Warning |
| Relation path's source in the view must correspond to its source in the model.ConditionIncorrect source. |  |  |
| Relation path's source and target in the view must correspond to its source and target in the model.ConditionBoth the target and the source are incorrect. |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00569: Data Functions

- page_id: `263094953`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/263094953/Data+Functions
- version_number: 20
- version_date: `2025-11-13T10:03:46.335+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model analysis > Functions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The table below lists the functions available in the *DataFunctions* namespace of the Standard Libraries.

- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- 
- ← **VEIKIA NETEISINGAI?**
- 
- 
- 
- ← **NEVEIKIA**
- 
- 
- 
- ]]>
- 
- =]]>
- 
- 
- 

| Function | Example | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ==abstract function == { in ref feature x[0..1] : DataValue in ref feature y[0..1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x == y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) false | Returns true if x and y are equal in value.Related: |
| ===function === { in ref feature x[0..1] : DataValue in ref feature y[0..1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x === y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) false | Returns true if x and y refer to the exact same element.Related: |
| additionabstract function + { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[0..1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x + y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 110 | Returns the sum of x and y.Related: |
| subtractionabstract function - { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[0..1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x - y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) -10 | Returns the difference between x and y.Related: |
| multiplicationabstract function * { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x * y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 3000 | Returns the product of x and y.Related: |
| divisionabstract function / { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x / y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 0.8333 | Returns the quotient of x divided by y.Related: |
| exponentiationabstract function ** { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x ** y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 2147483647 | Returns x raised to the power of y (exponentiation).Related: |
| Bitwise XORabstract function ^ { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x ^ y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 2147483647NEVEIKIA? EXPECTED RESULT: 00001110 in decimal = 14Currently works same as exponentiation (**) | Returns the bitwise exclusive OR (XOR) of x and y.Related: |
| modulusabstract function % { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x % y}attribute result = Example(50,60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) 50 | Returns the remainder of x divided by y (modulus).Related: |
| notabstract function not { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; not(x)}attribute switch = false;attribute result = Example(switch);Output>> result = Example(switch) true | Returns true if x is false, and false if x is true (inverts a Boolean value).Related: |
| xorabstract function xor { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x xor y }attribute switchOne = true;attribute switchTwo = false;attribute result = Example(switchOne, switchTwo);Output>> result = Example(switchOne, switchTwo) true | Returns true if either x or y is true, but not both.Related: |
| ~abstract function ~ { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; ~x}attribute switch = false;attribute result = Example(switch);Output>> result = Example(switch) trueNEVEIKIA - turetu veikti taip pat kaip not() ir duot output: true, bet actual output: Could not evaluate value of 'x' Attribute. Cause: No definition for '~' Function. | Returns true if x is false, and false if x is true (inverts a Boolean value). |
| Logical ORabstract function \| { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x \| y }attribute switchOne = true;attribute switchTwo = false;attribute result = Example(switchOne, switchTwo);Output>> result = Example(switchOne, switchTwo) true | Returns true if either x or y, or both, are true.Related: |
| &abstract function & { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x & y }attribute switchOne = true;attribute switchTwo = false;attribute result = Example(switchOne, switchTwo);Output>> result = Example(switchOne, switchTwo) false | Returns true if both x and y are true. Related: |
| less thanabstract function < { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x < y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) true | Returns true if x is less than y.Related: |
| greater thanabstract function > { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x > y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) false | Returns true if x is greater than y.Related:]]> |
| less than or equal toabstract function <= { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x <= y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) true | Returns true if x is less than or equal to y.Related: |
| greater than or equal toabstract function >= { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x >= y}attribute result = Example(50, 60);Output>> result = Example(50, 60) false | Returns true if x is greater than or equal to y.Related:=]]> |
| maxabstract function max { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; max(x, y)}attribute a = 50;attribute b = 60;attribute result = Example(a, b);Output>> result = Example(a, b) 60 | Returns the greater of x and y.Related: |
| minabstract function min { in ref feature x[1] : DataValue in ref feature y[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; min(x, y)}attribute a = 50;attribute b = 60;attribute result = Example(a, b);Output>> result = Example(a, b) 50 | Returns the lesser of x and y.Related: |
| ..abstract function .. { in ref feature lower[1] : DataValue in ref feature upper[1] : DataValue} | calc def Example { private import DataFunctions::**; in x; in y; x..y}attribute result = Example(10,15);Output>> result = Example(10, 15) 10 11 12 13 14 15 | Returns a sequence of values from x to y, inclusive. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577362 space=SYSML2P version=5 -->
## PAGE 00570: Dependency

- page_id: `249577362`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577362/Dependency
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:14:30.896+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Relations > Traceability
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A dependency is a kind of relationship between any number of client (source) and supplier (target) elements. This implies that a change to a supplier element may result in a change to a client element.Dependencies can be useful for representing relationships between elements in an abstract way.Dependencies can be binary, with one client and supplier, or n-ary, with one client and more than one supplier.The n-ary dependency is displayed as a dot symbol with segments consisting of dependency paths and client/supplier symbols.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays a *Use*dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source) and*Data Layer*is the supplier (target). The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary dependency where the package *Service Layer*is the client (source). The packages*Data Layer*and*External Interface Layer*are the supplier (target).The supplier end of the dependency path is indicated with an arrowhead.

Dependency is deleted if client or supplier count in the dependency is reduced zero.

##### Creating dependencies

###### Creating dependencies via the Textual Editor

To create dependencies via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the dependency keyword.
2. Specify the element name (optional), then the from keyword and the name of the client (source) element. Then follow with the keyword to and the name(s) of the supplier (target) element(s) listed in a comma-seperated list. If the dependency has no name, the keyword*from*can be omitted.
3. (Optional) Specify a body with any annotating elements owned by the dependency via annotation relationships.
4. Click the Synchronize button. package'Data Layer';package'Service Layer';package'External Interface Layer';dependencyfrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // unnamed dependencydependencyUsefrom'Service Layer'to'External Interface Layer'; // named dependencydependency from 'Service Layer' to 'External Interface Layer', 'Data Layer'; // unnamed n-ary dependency (with more than one supplier (target))dependency'Service Layer'to'Data Layer'{ // unnamed dependency with a body 
/* 'Service Layer' is the dependency client, 'Data Layer' is the supplier. */ 
}

###### Creating dependencies via the view palette

To create dependencies via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the **dependency** button.
2. In the view pane, click on the client (source) and then on the supplier (target) to create a dependency path between them.

###### Creating dependenciesvia the Create Relation command

To create dependencies via the Create Relation command

1. Right-click on the client (source) element.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Create Relation , then in the submenu, under Outgoing , click dependency . The Create... dialog opens.
3. In the Create... dialog, do one of the following:
  1. In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.
  2. Click the Add [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click OK .
  3. Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the Create... dialog.
4. In the Create... dialog, click OK .

###### Creating n-ary dependencies via the view

To create n-ary dependencies via the view

1. .
2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the client command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's client. Repeat for all the needed elements.
  2. Click the supplier command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's supplier. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary dependencies

###### Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the view

To convert binary dependency to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary dependency you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary dependency is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-arydependencytobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary dependency to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the dependency you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary dependency dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the dependency has one client and supplier.

Loading rewrite

style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">dependency</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Service Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-red,#ae2e24);">'Data Layer'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {  <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> <span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// unnamed dependency with a body</span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">/* 'Service Layer' is the dependency client, 'Data Layer' is the supplier. */</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Creating dependencies via the view palette</h4><p>To create dependencies via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Other Relationships group, click the<span> <strong>dependency</strong></span><strong> </strong><span>button.</span></li><li>In the view pane, click on the client (source) and then on the supplier (target) to create a dependency path between them.</li></ol><h4>Creating <span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">dependencies </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">via the Create Relation command</span></h4><p>To create dependencies via the Create Relation command</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click on the client (source) element.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Create Relation</strong>, then in the submenu, under <strong>Outgoing</strong>, click <strong>dependency</strong>. The <strong>Create... </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ol><li>In a view, click the element symbol you want to be the supplier (target). Hold Shift to select multiple elements.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>button. The element selection dialog opens. In the element selection dialog, select the element you want to be the supplier (target) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Drag and drop the needed element you want to be the supplier (target) from the Containment tree onto the <strong>Create... </strong>dialog.</li></ol></li><li>In the <strong>Create...</strong><strong> </strong>dialog, click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><h4>Creating n-ary dependencies via the view</h4><p>To create n-ary dependencies via the view</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the diagram"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Convert a binary dependency to a n-ary]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:</span><ol><li>Click the<span> </span><strong>client<span> </span></strong>command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's client. Repeat for all the needed elements.</li><li>Click the<span> </span><strong>supplier<span> </span></strong>command. Click on the element symbol you want to specify as the dependency's supplier. Repeat for all the needed elements.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Converting binary and n-ary dependencies</h3><h4>Converting binary dependency to n-ary via the view</h4><p>To convert binary dependency to n-ary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the binary dependency you want to convert to an n-ary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to N-ary Dot</strong>. The binary dependency is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.</li></ol><h4>Converting <span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">n-ary </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">dependency </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">to </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">binary </span><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">via the view</span></h4><p>To convert n-ary dependency to binary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the dependency you want to convert to a binary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to Path</strong>. The n-ary dependency dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the dependency has one client and supplier. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout><div style="width: 1.0px;height: 1.0px;"><div><p>Loading rewrite</p></div></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577400 space=SYSML2P version=7 -->
## PAGE 00571: Derive requirement

- page_id: `249577400`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577400/Derive+requirement
- version_number: 7
- version_date: `2025-12-11T19:26:46.277+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Requirements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Derived requirements are created using the derive relationship via the derivation connection. The derive relationship links more detailed requirements to their original, higher-level requirements. A derivation connection asserts that one or more derived requirements are derived from a single original requirement. The single end for the original requirement should subset the original requirement, while the rest of the ends should subset derived requirements. Once the original requirement is satisfied, the derived ones must also be satisfied.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

Derivation is deleted if original or derived requirement count in the derivation is reduced zero.

##### Deriving requirements

###### Deriving requirements via the Textual Editor

To derive requirements via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the derivation and import the RequirementDerivation library.
2. Declare the #derivation conncetion keyword, specify the derivation name (optional), [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] (optional).
3. In the derivation body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reference subsetting' space=''] the derived requirement. Repeat for multiple derived requirements.
4. Click the Synchronize button.

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in separate packages

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling; 
} 
packageDerivations{// derivations package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
privateimportRequirements::*;// requirements package import 
#derivationconnection : 'Derived System Requirements'{// unnamed specialized (feature typing) binary derivation connection 
end#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement end 
end#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement end 
} 
}

// --------- requirements and requirement derivations in the same package

packageRequirements{// requirements package 
privateimportRequirementDerivation::*;// *RequirementDerivation* library import 
requirement'heating & cooling'; 
requirementheating; 
requirementcooling;#derivationconnection'heating & cooling derivations'{// named n-ary derivation connectionend#original::>'heating & cooling';// original requirement endend#derive::>'heating';// derived requirement endend#derive::>'cooling';// derived requirement end}}

###### Deriving requirements via the view palette

To derive requirements via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click one of the following:
  1. The **derivation** button to create a derivation connection.
    1. In the view pane, click on the requirement you want to specify as the original one, then the one you want to specify as derived.
  2. The **n-ary****derivation** button to create an n-ary derivation connection.
    1. Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary derivation dot symbol to be created.
    2. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **original** end command.
    3. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original one.
    4. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the **derived** end command.
    5. Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived one. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.

If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation**,**n-ary derivation**,****or the**derivation****def**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.

###### Creating additional n-ary derivations via the view

To create additional n-ary derivations via the view

1. Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:
  1. Click the original end command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original requirement.
  2. Click the **derived end** command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived requirement. Repeat for all the needed elements.

##### Converting binary and n-ary derivations

###### Converting binary derivation to n-ary via the view

To convert binary derivation to n-ary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the binary derivation you want to convert to an n-ary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to N-ary Dot . The binary derivation is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.

###### Converting n-aryderivationtobinaryvia the view

To convert n-ary derivation to binary via the view

1. In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the derivation you want to convert to a binary.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Refactor , then Convert to Path . The n-ary derivation dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed. The **Convert to Path**command is only available if the derivation has one client and supplier.

##### Creating derivation connection definition elements

###### Creating derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

To create derivation definitions via the Textual Editor

1. In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the #derivation connection def keyword.
2. Name the element, then specify the derivation definition features in the element body:
  1. Specify the end #original keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] of the original requirement.
  2. Specify the end #derive keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specializations' space=''] of the derived requirement.
3. Click the **Synchronize**button. #derivationconnectiondef'Derived system requirements'{ 
end#originaloriginalReq:vehicleRequirements; 
end#derivederivedReq:engineRequirements; 
}

###### Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands

- See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extract Definition and Usage' space='']page.

###### Creating derivation definitions via the view palette

To create derivation definitions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the **derivation** **def** button to create a derivation definition element. If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the **derivation** or**n-ary derivation**button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.
2. Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
