                        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// derived requirement end<br /></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">derive</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> ::&gt; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">'cooling'</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;                                                </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// derived requirement end<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    } <br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Deriving requirements via the view palette</h4><p>To derive requirements via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click one of the following:<br /><ol><li>The<span> <strong>derivation </strong></span>button to create a derivation connection.<ol><li>In the view pane, click on the requirement you want to specify as the original one, then the one you want to specify as derived.</li></ol></li><li>The<span> <strong>n-ary </strong><strong>derivation</strong></span><span> </span>button to create an n-ary derivation connection.<ol><li>Click in the view pane where you want the n-ary derivation dot symbol to be created.</li><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the<span> <strong>original </strong></span><strong>end<span> </span></strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original one.</li><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, click the<span> <strong>derived </strong></span><strong>end<span> </span></strong>command. </li><li>Draw the end from the n-ary connection symbol to the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived one. Repeat for all elements you want to connect.</li></ol></li></ol></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="208c68ea-9cec-45e6-a15b-dd18b8d3f1f3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <span><strong>derivation</strong>,<strong> n-ary derivation</strong>,<strong> </strong></span>or the<span> <strong>derivation </strong></span><strong>def<span> </span></strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Creating additional n-ary derivations via the view</h4><p>To create additional n-ary derivations via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol and in its smart manipulator, do one of the following:<ol><li>Click the<span> </span><strong>original end<span> </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the original requirement. </li><li>Click the<span> <strong>derived end</strong></span><strong><span> </span></strong>command. Click on the requirement symbol you want to specify as the derived requirement. Repeat for all the needed elements.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Converting binary and n-ary derivations</h3><h4>Converting binary derivation to n-ary via the view</h4><p>To convert binary derivation to n-ary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the binary derivation you want to convert to an n-ary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to N-ary Dot</strong>. The binary derivation is converted and the n-ary dot symbol is displayed.</li></ol><h4>Converting <span>n-ary </span><span>derivation </span><span>to </span><span>binary </span><span>via the view</span></h4><p>To convert n-ary derivation to binary via the view</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view pane, right-click the n-ary dot symbol of the derivation you want to convert to a binary.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Refactor</strong>, then <strong>Convert to Path</strong>. The n-ary derivation dot symbol is converted and the binary path is displayed.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0dcc5e6d-b75e-4dc3-9a76-b8575964a7c5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Convert to Path </strong>command is only available if the derivation has one client and supplier. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Creating derivation connection definition elements</h3><h4>Creating derivation definitions via the Textual Editor</h4><p>To create derivation definitions via the Textual Editor</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Textual Editor, place the cursor where you want to create the element and declare the <em>#derivation connection def</em> keyword.</li><li>Name the element, then specify the derivation definition features in the element body:<ol><li>Specify the <em>end</em> <em>#original </em>keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the original requirement. </li><li>Specify the <em>end</em> <em>#derive </em>keyword, followed by the end name (optional) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specializations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specialization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the derived requirement. </li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Synchronize </strong>button. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="802b05e2-76b4-4c33-bb2a-fa228b066a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">#derivation </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connection</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">'Derived system requirements' </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">{</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #original </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">originalReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">vehicleRequirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">end</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> #derive </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">derivedReq</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">engineRequirements</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4>Auto-creating derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</h4><p><span>To auto-create derivation definition elements via the Extract Definition commands</span></p><hr /><ul><li><span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">See the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Extract Definition and Usage" /></ac:link></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">page.</span></span></li></ul><h4>Creating derivation definitions via the view palette</h4><p>To create derivation definitions via the view palette</p><hr /><ol><li>In the view palette, under the Connectors group, click the<span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>derivation</strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> <strong>def </strong></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">button to create a derivation definition element.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35819f6b-9a74-40cd-bfc8-eac27c667eb0"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the needed button is not displayed, click the down-facing arrow next to the <strong>derivation</strong> or<strong> n-ary derivation </strong>button and select the needed button in the dropdown menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click in the view pane where you want to create the derivation definition symbol. Specify the derivation definition ends (via the Textual Editor or the Specification panel).</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577543 space=SYSML2P version=4 -->
## PAGE 00572: Directed features for actions

- page_id: `249577543`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577543/Directed+features+for+actions
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2026-04-23T16:23:26.163+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > Actions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Actions may have features with directions in, out or inout that are considered to be parameters of the action. Features with direction in or inout are input parameters, and features with direction out or inout are output parameters. An action usage inherits the parameters of its definitions, if any, and it can also define its own parameters or redefine those of its definitions. Directed features may also be bound to indicate the equivalence of their values.

##### Specifying directed features for actions

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for actions via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the action's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name. actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; // *in* directed feature 
outpicture:Picture; // *out* directed feature}

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the view palette

To specify directed features for actions via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the action for which you want to create the element.

###### Specifying directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for actions via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select an action symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

##### Binding directed features

Directed features can also bebound. An output parameter of one action usage may be bound to the input parameter of another action usage. Such a binding indicates that the values of the target input parameter will always be the same as the values of the source output parameter. For more information on binding, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding' space=''] page.

actiondefTakePicture{ 
inscene:Scene; 
outpicture:Picture;

// binding *in*parameters TakePicture.scene with focus.scene 
bindscene=focus.scene; 
 
actionfocus:Focus{inscene;outimage; } 
firstfocusthenshoot; 
flowfocus.imagetoshoot.image; 
actionshoot:Shoot{inimage;outpicture; } 
 
// binding *out*parameters TakePicture.picture with shoot.picture 
bindpicture=shoot.picture; 
}

The binding of action parameters does not model the case when there is an actual transfer of items between actions. Transfers can be modeled using flows, succession flows as well as send and accept actions. For more information, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transfers between actions' space=''] page.

##### Redefining parameters

Each owned parameter implicitly redefines the corresponding inherited parameter according to the latter's order:

50%

actiondefImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId; 
incustomerName; 
incustomerEmail; 
} 
actionimportCustomerData:ImportCustomerData{ 
incustomerId;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerId 
incustomerName;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerName 
incustomerEmail;// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerEmail 
incustomerPhoneNumber;// new owned parameter 
}

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you redefine the inherited parameters via either of the following procedures:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Redefinition' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Redefinition' space='']

The redefinitions are created as follows:

- If there are other inherited parameters from the same owner with preceding positions, then all of those parameters are redefined. E.g., if there are three inherited parameters and you redefine the second, then the first and the second parameters are redefined, while the third is unaffected. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 2.png']
- The declared name for each redefining parameter is derived from the redefined parameter name. If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters customerId and validationId are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name. [ATTACHMENT filename='parameter redefinition 3.png']
- The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.

For more information, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transfers between actions" /></ac:link> page.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Redefining parameters</h3><p>Each owned parameter implicitly redefines the corresponding inherited parameter according to the latter's order:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="492cc2f4-0db2-40d4-9ab8-4b15882d34fd"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58de3915-9c04-4e2e-86c7-d7f37a3e5b15"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6af31a20-6191-498a-a3bc-51a088203c2f"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">def</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ImportCustomerData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerId</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span>in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerEmail</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">action</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">importCustomerData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">ImportCustomerData</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerId</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerId</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerName</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerName</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerEmail</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// implicitly redefines ImportCustomerData::customerEmail</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">in</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">customerPhoneNumber</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">; </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-green-bolder,#164b35);">// new owned parameter</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b571cff3-9ab8-4b82-a9f4-859230621257"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 1.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>If you redefine the inherited parameters via either of the following procedures:</p><ul><li data-uuid="56d489d9-39be-4cc2-ba6d-f0e36365f2d3"><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefining a usage via the symbol shortcut menu in a view]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li data-uuid="4a3d7919-8f9c-42ef-85f6-f4498247ba15"><ac:link ac:anchor="Redefining a usage via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Redefinition" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Redefining a usage via the Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p>The redefinitions are created as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3514ff67-21fd-48ec-a9a4-d486e4f47c74">If there are other inherited parameters from the same owner with preceding positions, then all of those parameters are redefined. E.g., if there are three inherited parameters and you redefine the second, then the first and the second parameters are redefined, while the third is unaffected. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 2.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="67c86a94-c775-477b-b6e6-bc1962259098">The declared name for each redefining parameter is derived from the redefined parameter name. If multiple parameters are redefined, the names are concatenated. E.g., parameters <em>customerId </em>and <em>validationId </em>are inherited via separate specializations from two different definitions. Both parameters represent the first position in the order of inherited parameters. Redefining either of them creates a single redefining parameter with a concatenated name.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter redefinition 3.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="ddd4d155-cb21-4413-9a36-a6fda316b0c6">The direction of each created parameter is the same as, or compatible with, the direction of the redefined parameter.</li></ul><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577630 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00573: Directed features for states

- page_id: `249577630`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577630/Directed+features+for+states
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:43:27.172+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Behavior > States
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Specify features with directions *in*, *out,*or *inout*for states that act as the parameters of the state. Directed features can also be [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding' space=''].

##### Specifying directed features for states

###### Specifying directed features for states via the Textual Editor

To specify directed features for states via the Textual Editor

- In the Textual Editor, within the state's body, specify the needed direction keyword in or out, followed by the needed feature name.

statedefVehicleStates{inoperatingVehicle:Vehicle;}

###### Specifying directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

To specify directed features for states via the smart manipulator in a view

- In a view, select a state symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the in ref or out ref command.

###### Specifying directed features for states via the view palette

To specify directed features for states via the view palette

1. In the view palette, under the Actions group, click one of the following:
  1. The **in ref** button.
  2. The **out ref** button.
2. Hover the cursor over the view pane and click on the state for which you want to create the element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577796 space=SYSML2P version=22 -->
## PAGE 00574: Displaying elements in symbolic views

- page_id: `249577796`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577796/Displaying+elements+in+symbolic+views
- version_number: 22
- version_date: `2026-06-18T11:49:21.995+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use the Display commands to display element symbols in a graphical representation of a symbolic view.

##### Display commands

| Display command category and its commands | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Nested-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the nested-view . Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as nested symbols. |
| Display Features | The command displays owned and inherited features of any metatype.Displays subject and actor parameters for requirements and cases as element symbols. To display them as shapes on border, use the Display Parameters command. |
| Display Features Recursively | The command displays all owned and inherited features of any metatype recursively. |
| Display Parts | The command displays owned and inherited parts. |
| Display Parts Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited parts recursively. |
| Display Actions | The command displays owned and inherited actions. |
| Display Actions Recursively | The command displays owned and inherited actions recursively. |
| Display Swimlanes | The command displays owned and inherited swimlane symbols, and any element symbols related to each swimlane.The command is only available for specific and . |
| Display Owned Members | The command displays owned members based on the 'ownedMember' property's value.The command is only available for . |
| Display Owned Members Recursively | The command displays owned members recursively based on the 'ownedMember' property's value.The command is only available for . |
| Tree-view style | The category contains commands that render elements in the tree-view .Owned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as symbols related via Feature Membership relationship paths.Contains all the same commands as the Nested-view style category, except for Display Actions, Display Actions Recursively, Display Swimlanes.The Display Features command displays connectors between features only in the nested-view style. |
| Relations | The category contains commands for displaying relation paths of the selected symbol. |
| Display Relations | The command displays:paths of all types of connectors (connection, interface, succession, binding, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. Connector paths can also be .paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.paths of other relations (annotation, dependency, type featuring, import, and all memberships) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.paths that exist between symbols of an element that owns or inherits features of the applicable model element (see below) and the referenced element connected via reference subsetting.Applicable elements: , , , , , , , , , frame concern usage, .Example... |
| Display Relations Excluding Specializations | The command displays all the same paths as the Display Relations command, excluding paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.). |
| Display In-Between Relations | The command displays all the same paths as the Display Relations command, but only between the selected element symbols.This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view. |
| Display In-Between Relations Excluding Specializations | The command displays all the same paths as the Display Relations command, but only between the selected element symbols, excluding paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.).This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view. |
| Other | The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols. |
| Display Ports | The command displays owned and inherited ports.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent. |
| Display Parameters | The command displays owned and inherited directional features.The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.Displays subject and actor parameters for requirements and cases as shapes on border. To display them as element symbols, use the Display Features command. |
| Display Ends | The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.The command is only available for . |
| Display Exposed Elements | The command displays elements exposed for the view. Exposed elements depend on the (e.g., recursivity) and . The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a .This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an . |

#### NOTE: Recursive commands

Recursive commands

Recursive commands are indicated by the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the *drone in SAR mission*part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.

There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the **Display**commands to be available (except for the **Display Exposed Elements**command).

##### Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)Displaying element symbols in a view

###### Displaying element symbols

To display element symbols

1. In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display. To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.Use the **Select**buttons in the **Select**option group in the view panel.
2. Do one of the following:
  1. Click the Display button [ATTACHMENT filename='display_button.png'] in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.
  2. Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select Display . Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.

##### Displaying connectors in a view

In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol using the**Display Relations**command. See theprocedure.

###### Proxy connectors

Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via the feature chain.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

50%

partwheelHubAssembly:WheelHubAssembly{ 
partlugBoltJoints:LugBoltJoint[0..5]; 
partwheel:WheelAssembly[1] { 
partw:Wheel[1] { 
partrim:TireMountingRim[2]; 
partmountingHoles:LugBoltMountingHole[5]; 
} 
} 
connect[0..1]lugBoltJointsto[1]wheel.w.mountingHoles; 
}

###### In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part *lugBoltJoints*and part *mountingHoles* nested within part *w*, which is nested within the part *wheel*. 
Since the nested part symbols*w*and*mountingHoles*are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between*lugBoltJoints*and*wheel*, which is a displayed parent element symbol of*w*and*mountingHoles.*To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part*wheel*and the connector path.A connector label with the*w.mountingHoles*feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to.

###### Elaborating a connector

The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.

To elaborate/simplify a connector

1. Do one of the following:
  1. To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Elaborate [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is elaborated.
  2. To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the Simplify [IMAGE alt='' src=''] command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Connector end labels

In a view, the connector path ends are displayed with labels containing the information regarding each connector end. The label may include:

- Metadata 
e.g. *«#CustomReqMetadata»*
- Name or proxy connector end '<'declared short name'> 'declared name' ::> 'proxy connection end'; e.g. <END_1.1> org ::> .vehicle mass
- Multiplicity 
e.g. *[1]*
- Metadata values 
e.g: *{author = "John"; assignee = "Victor";}*

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Displaying n-ary connectors in a view

In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.

The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='N-ary connection' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='N-ary interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Derive requirement' space='']

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency' space=''] can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths between the selected elements and the n-ary dot symbol via the **Display Relations**command.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and*heating*is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

50%

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement *heating & cooling*is the original and *heating*is the derived one.The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.

###### Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view

To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view

- Select the containing element symbol and click the Display command, then in its dropdown menu, click Display Features . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view

To display n-ary connector ends in a view

- Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the Display command. In its dropdown menu, click Display Ends . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png']

###### Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view

- Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the Display command, then Display Relations . [ATTACHMENT filename='Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png']

##### Showing a tooltip for ports and parameters

Hovering over a feature represented as a shape on border (e.g., ports, parameters), a tooltip is shown, displaying both owned and inherited attributes and items.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

/><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[include use cases]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">   Display Owned Members Recursively</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned members recursively based on the 'ownedMember' property's value.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c94082d4-7e6b-409e-9d3d-da42cc1774fd"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command is only available for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Package" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[packages]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Tree-view style</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><span>The category contains commands that render elements in the tree-view </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Rendering views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering style]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p><p title=""><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">O</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">wned and inherited features of the selected element symbol are represented as symbols related via Feature Membership relationship paths.</span></p><ul title=""><li data-uuid="6c51ea38-75c0-4f4f-98de-b673abe64f18"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Contains all the same commands as the <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Nested-view style </strong>category, except for Display Actions, Display Actions Recursively, Display Swimlanes.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="a943e155-e603-435d-8ab2-29b9ef148836"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The <strong>Display Features</strong> command displays connectors between features only in the nested-view style.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title=""><strong>Relations</strong></span></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">The category contains commands for displaying relation paths of the selected symbol.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Relations</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">paths of all types of connectors (connection, interface, succession, binding, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view. Connector paths can also be <ac:link ac:anchor="Elaborating a connector"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[elaborated]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="143e589f-4ebb-4617-bb39-180214400a01"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.</span></span></li><li data-uuid="9a0ac894-22e5-482e-ba42-724f5880562c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><span>paths of other relations (annotation, dependency, type featuring, import, and all memberships) that exist between the selected element symbol and any others displayed in the view.</span></span></span></li><li data-uuid="143e589f-4ebb-4617-bb39-180214400a01"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>paths that exist between symbols of an element that owns or inherits features of the applicable model element (see below) and the referenced element connected via reference subsetting.</span></span><ul><li data-uuid="98c3d75c-0a28-4ec4-8149-79467f804463"><span>Applicable elements: </span><em><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Occurrences" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[event occurrence usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Perform action" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[perform action usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Include use case" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[include use case usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exhibit state" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exhibit state usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Satisfy requirement" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[satisfy requirement usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying requirement verification usages for verification case objective" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[requirement verification usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assume constraint" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[assume constraint usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Require constraint" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[require constraint usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Assert constraint" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[assert constraint usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, </span></em><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Frame concern" /><ac:link-body><em>frame</em> <em>concern usage</em></ac:link-body></ac:link></span><em><span>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Rendering views basics" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[rendering usage]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /></span></em><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17a138b4-b209-49de-8fe4-cdef8abb6186"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_references.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Relations Excluding Specializations</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays all the same paths as the <strong>Display Relations</strong> command, excluding </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.).</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>   Display In-Between Relations</td><td><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">all the same paths as the <strong>Display Relations</strong> command, but only between the selected element symbols</span>.</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="47e56910-677d-413e-9ef7-e0a7212f104c"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td>   Display In-Between Relations Excluding Specializations</td><td><p><span>The command displays <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">all the same paths as the <strong>Display Relations</strong> command, but only between the selected element symbols, </span></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">excluding </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">paths of all types of specializations (subclassification, feature typing, subsetting, etc.).</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="e5bfb3dd-44b1-419c-a46c-1ee8b96f5497"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);" title="">This command is available only when at least two symbols are selected in a view.</span></li></ul></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><strong title="">Other</strong></td><td class="highlight-grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><span title="">The category contains commands for displaying any other element symbols.</span></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ports</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited ports.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea0381f5-1e71-42b5-8879-4601ea095343"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Parameters </td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays owned and inherited directional features.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71db2b8d-7fa0-4306-8ff7-8971ca47c54b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="c0433290-6aaa-4b4e-9a3e-897df77bb988"><span>The command's availability is element metatype-dependent.</span></li><li data-uuid="63307655-e75f-4177-a062-15c5c6dafb1b"><span>Displays subject and actor parameters for requirements and cases as shapes on border. To display them as element symbols, use the <strong>Display Features</strong> command.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td>   Display Ends</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command displays end segments of the n-ary connection and the n-ary interface symbols.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="18987458-ba47-4c34-847d-ee44388c3570"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">The command is only available for <ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying n-ary connectors in a view"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[n-ary dot symbols]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><span>   Display Exposed Elements</span></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>The command displays elements exposed for the view. <span>Exposed elements depend on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[exposition kind]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (e.g., recursivity) and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Filtering elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[filters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7866e435-7592-4369-ade6-4bcb219fed2e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="4acb477e-c9ee-4f2b-9122-83b716c28268"><span>The command is available when invoked for the diagram pane or a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Docked symbols" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[docked symbol]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li data-uuid="18dd3bff-bbf5-408d-a8d2-3c366eeee6eb"><p>This command is also available via the hyperlink in the notification displayed on any symbolic view containing an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Exposing elements for views" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[expose relationship]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display exposed elements notification.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f9bdb1c-48d3-4406-b1a5-fc676442b857"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Recursive commands</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Recursive commands are indicated by the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_button.png" /></ac:image> recursive command button displayed next to the non-recursive command.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursive_cmd.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_commands.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Display Parts command is used to display parts owned by the <em>drone in SAR mission </em>part. On the left, the command is executed in the nested-view style, while the right shows the tree-view style.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="722af941-19b0-4426-a909-ef6eb0ae1b9b"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span>There must be at least one element symbol already displayed in a view for the <strong>Display </strong>commands to be available (except for the <strong>Display Exposed Elements </strong>command).</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd72916b-65f7-44c1-ba2b-3b9ed1c54c30"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Displaying element symbols in a diagram with symbol(s)</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Displaying element symbols in a view</h3><h4>Displaying element symbols</h4><p>To display element symbols</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">In a view, select the specific symbol(s) whose features, connectors, etc., you want to display.<br /></span><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1c2dfbd-2cfc-428a-b545-87f6715e5c90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0f41dad1-5ad0-45d3-a18e-743b57ba085f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To select multiple symbols, use the following shortcuts...</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>Hold Shift+Left Mouse Button on each symbol to select multiple symbols at once.</li><li>Press Ctrl+A to select all symbols on the docked symbol. </li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+B to select sibling symbols.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+S to select symbols of the same type.</li><li>Select a symbol in the view and press Ctrl+Alt+C to select connected symbols.</li><li>Hold down the Left Mouse Button anywhere in the view pane and drag to select multiple symbols.</li><li>Use the <strong>Select </strong>buttons in the <strong>Select </strong>option group in the view panel.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Do one of the following:</span></p><ol><li>Click the <strong>Display</strong> button <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_button.png" /></ac:image> in the view toolbar. Click the appropriate display command in the dropdown menu.</li><li>Right-click the element symbol(s). In the shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong>. Click the appropriate display command in the submenu.</li></ol></li></ol><h3>Displaying connectors in a view</h3><p>In a view, a connector (connection, bind, flow, interface, succession, succession flow) can be displayed as a path symbol <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">using the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Display Relations </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">command. S</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">ee the </span><ac:link ac:anchor="Displaying element symbols" /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"> procedure.</span></p><h4><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Proxy connectors</span></h4><p>Nested elements that are connected via connectors may be displayed via proxy connector points. The proxy connector point symbol at the intersection between an element symbol and a connector indicates that the element to which the connector is connected is nested deeper within the element to which the proxy connector point symbol is attached. The connector label indicates the element to which the connector is connected via the feature chain.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a5bc18e-4ea8-44b6-b1ad-8d1b0b980e90"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="28baaa67-c188-4eed-811b-ebb046e222bd"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Proxy_connectors.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d1776f58-016f-4488-b406-dca75f670789"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="544bb35c-59cb-48bf-978e-4d57ff4349f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelHubAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltJoint</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">WheelAssembly</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">Wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">] {</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">rim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">TireMountingRim</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">2</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">            </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">part</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> : </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-teal,#206a83);">LugBoltMountingHole</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">5</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">];</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">        }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    }</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">    </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">connect<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">0</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">.1</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">]</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">lugBoltJoints</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue,#0055cc);">to</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> [<span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-green-bolder,#1f845a);">1</span>] </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">wheel</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">w</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">.</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text-accent-blue-bolder,#09326c);">mountingHoles</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">;</span><br /><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">}</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h6 style="text-align: center;">In the textual notation, a connection connects two parts: part <em>lugBoltJoints </em>and part <em>mountingHoles</em> nested within part <em>w</em>, which is nested within the part <em>wheel</em>.<br /><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Since the nested part symbols </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">are not displayed in the view, the proxy connector path is drawn between </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">lugBoltJoints </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">, which is a displayed parent element symbol of </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> and </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">mountingHoles.<br /></em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To represent that the connector is connected to a nested element, a proxy connector point symbol is displayed at the intersection of the part </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">wheel </em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">and the connector path.<br /></span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">A connector label with the </span><em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">w.mountingHoles</em><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> feature chain informs you which element symbol the connector is connected to. </span></h6><h4><span style="letter-spacing: -0.003em;">Elaborating a connector</span></h4><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The connector path symbol can be additionally elaborated for improved visualization of the connector and its related elements.</span></p><p>To elaborate/simplify a connector</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>To elaborate a connector in a view, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Elaborate <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="elaborate_button.png" /></ac:image><span style="color:var(--ds-background-accent-red-bolder,#c9372c);"> </span></strong>command. The connector is elaborated.</li><li>To simplfiy a connector into a path symbol only, click the connector symbol, and in its smart manipulator, click the <strong>Simplify <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="simplify_button.png" /></ac:image> </strong>command. The connector is simplified into a path symbol only.</li></ol></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Elaborating_a_connector.png" /></ac:image></p><h4>Connector end labels</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>In a view, the connector path ends are displayed with labels containing the information regarding each connector end. The label may include:</span></span></p><ul><li data-uuid="9533f342-06f8-4af0-ba19-3e010d6f4e30"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Metadata<br />e.g. <em>«#CustomReqMetadata»</em><br /></span></span></li><li data-uuid="a7f4b690-e159-4497-8d2f-6584a9d32955"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Name or proxy connector end<br /></span></span>'&lt;'declared short name'&gt; 'declared name' ::&gt; 'proxy connection end'; e.g. <em>&lt;END_1.1&gt; org ::&gt; .vehicle mass</em></li><li data-uuid="482d9ce8-c81e-42ae-8b5f-6d189345d0fa"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Multiplicity<br />e.g. <em>[1]</em><br /></span></span></li><li data-uuid="95037af4-d39b-400f-bfb6-8ac87bf4eb11"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span>Metadata values <br />e.g: <em>{author = &quot;John&quot;; assignee = &quot;Victor&quot;;}</em> </span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="connector_end_labels.png" /></ac:image></span></span></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Displaying n-ary connectors in a view</h3><p>In addition to binary connectors, you can also have n-ary connectors. <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">An n-ary connector is a complex connector between three or more elements. The n-ary connector is displayed as a dot symbol with segments connecting the ends of the connector.</span></p><p>The following binary connectors can also be n-ary:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="N-ary connection" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connection]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="N-ary interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Derive requirement" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Derivation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="13f43002-a869-49a2-b0fa-21b855d3bb07"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dependency" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[dependency]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> can also be n-ary or binary. However, as it is not a connector, the following procedures do not apply to it. Dependencies can be displayed as paths between the selected elements and the n-ary dot symbol via the <strong>Display Relations </strong>command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><ac:structured-macro ac:name="section" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6bcd8a03-8277-409e-8ebf-4ac42d0493c0"><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a4d778b-bd2c-46b8-a56a-5b09282ce5c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed binary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and </span><em>heating </em><span>is the derived one. The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c339a57-b3ef-4d56-8b0d-79d86eb7fc5d"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">50%</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="n-ary_derivation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The image displays an unnamed n-ary derivation where the requirement <em>heating &amp; cooling </em><span>is the original and <em>heating </em>is the derived one. </span><span>The original and derived ends are connected with the connection path.</span></h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h4>Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary dot and its ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the containing element symbol and click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then in its dropdown menu, click <strong>Display Features</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary dot and its ends in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connector ends in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the n-ary dot symbol in the view, then click the <strong>Display</strong> command. In its dropdown menu, click <strong>Display Ends</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connector ends in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h4>Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</h4><p>To display n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view</p><hr /><ul><li>Select the symbol whose n-ary connectors you want to display. Click the <strong>Display </strong>command, then <strong>Display Relations</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying n-ary connectors for an element symbol in a view.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h3>Showing a tooltip for ports and parameters </h3><p>Hovering over a <span>feature represented as a shape on border (e.g., ports, parameters), a tooltip is shown, displaying both owned and inherited attributes and items.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Showing a tooltip for ports and parameters .png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577835 space=SYSML2P version=17 -->
## PAGE 00575: Displaying elements in tabular views

- page_id: `249577835`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577835/Displaying+elements+in+tabular+views
- version_number: 17
- version_date: `2026-05-06T19:06:10.255+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model composition > Views > Displaying elements in views
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Display elements in diagrammatic representations of tabular views.

Tabular views display up to 50,000 rows. If building a table exceeds this limit, the tool displays a notification suggesting that you narrow the table scope or adjust the display mode settings and try again.

##### Displaying elements in tabular views

To display elements in tabular views

1. Ensure the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating tabular views' space=''] view has:
  1. Tabular [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rendering views' space=''] .
  2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exposing elements for views' space=''] .
  3. (Optional) [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering elements for views' space=''] .
2. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Opening views' space=''] the view. The exposed and filtered elements are displayed in the tabular view.
3. (Optional) To reflect any changes in the model, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_table_button.png'] Refresh button in the view toolbar to update the view.

##### Managing table elements display mode

The table elements' display mode allows you to specify how the exposed elements should be displayed in the table. The default display mode for all tables is **List**. The display mode can be managed via the Textual Editor using dedicated attributes or the table toolbar's **Options** command. See the table below, indicating the display mode options available via the table toolbar's **Options** command and the corresponding attributes used in the textual notation.

| Display Mode (Options command) | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Complete Tree | Shows each element with all of its owners (direct, indirect, and common).Groups elements under common owners, including those higher up in the ownership hierarchy. |
| Compact Tree | Shows each element with its direct owners.Groups elements by their common owners when multiple elements share the same one. The omitted indirect owners are part of the common owner path's qualified name, e.g., indirectOwner::commonOwner. Omits indirect owners higher in the hierarchy. |
| List | Displays the exposed elements as a plain list.List is the default display mode for all tables. |

#### NOTE: Ownership explanation

Ownership explanation

- Direct owner: the immediate owner of an element.
- Indirect owner: an owner higher in the hierarchy (not immediate).
- Common owner: the same element that owns multiple exposed elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Managing table elements display mode via the Options command

To manage table elements' display mode via the Options command

- In the tabular view's toolbar, click the Options[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button, and in the dropdown menu, click the needed display mode option: **Complete Tree**, **Compact Tree**, **List**. The table elements are displayed in the selected display mode.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577060 space=SYSML2P version=2 -->
## PAGE 00577: Docked symbols

- page_id: `249577060`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577060/Docked+symbols
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-19T15:16:42.157+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Model management > Working with views > Working with symbols
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In SysML v2, an element symbol can be docked to a view, acting as a context and a kind of frame for a view.

**Docked symbol details:**

- The element represented as the docked symbol is the owner of elements whose symbols are contained by the docked symbol.
- Element symbols cannot be created outside the docked symbols' shape in the view pane. Creating element symbols outside the docked symbol's frame expands the frame to contain the new symbols.
- A view can have only one docked symbol.
- The docked element symbol can be undocked and vice versa. See the procedures below.
- Executing the 'Select in Containment tree' command:
  - on the docked symbol's body - selects the corresponding view.
  - on the docked symbol's header - selects the corresponding element .

##### Docking a symbol to a view

To dock a symbol to a view

- To create a view with a docked symbol:
  - In the Containment tree, right-click a usage or a definition element and in the shortcut menu, click **Create view**.
- To dock a symbol displayed in a view:
  - Select the element symbol in the view and click the **Dock** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button on the corner of the element frame. The **Dock**button is available only if the view displays one element symbol (e.g., the docking option is not available if there are two part usage symbols displayed in the view).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Docking a symbol to a view

##### Undocking a symbol from a view

To undock a symbol from a view

- Right-click the header of the docked symbol and in the shortcut menu, select **Refactor**, then **Undock**. Right-clicking anywhere but the header of the docked symbol will not display the **Undock**command in the shortcut menu.Once you undock a symbol, it remains the same size, taking up the entire view pane. Click the **Make Preferred Size** [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to shrink the symbol, allowing for more space in the view pane.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Undocking a symbol from a view

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Click the <strong>Make Preferred Size</strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Make_Preferred_Size_button.png" /></ac:image> button to shrink the symbol, allowing for more space in the view pane.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><h6 class="auto-cursor-target" style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Undocking_a_symbol_from_a_diagram.png" /></ac:image>Undocking a symbol from a view</h6>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249577080 space=SYSML2P version=11 -->
## PAGE 00578: Drag and Drop handler

- page_id: `249577080`
- space_key: `SYSML2P`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSML2P/pages/249577080/Drag+and+Drop+handler
- version_number: 11
- version_date: `2026-07-14T08:50:08.320+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML v2 Plugin Documentation > Element management > Working with elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands.

##### Using the Drag and Drop handler

To use the Drag and Drop handler

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.
  2. In a view:
    1. Select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the Drag and Drop handler [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_and_drop_handler.png'] .
    2. Select element symbols, then click and hold the Drag and Drop handler [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_and_drop_handler.png'] displayed next to one of the selected symbols. Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element ownership change' space='']. To take advantage of the functionalities provided by the Drag and Drop handler, make sure to drag the handler, not the symbol.
2. Drag and drop the element/symbol on:
  1. The target element symbol in a view.
  2. The appropriate field in the Specification panel. You can only drag an element symbol onto another element symbol in a view or the appropriate field in the Specification panel, never onto an element in the Containment tree. The latter modifies the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element ownership change' space=''].You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification panel' space=''] page.
3. The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.

##### Drag and Drop handler commands

Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols.

| Command name | Source | Target |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set Type>]]>If an element already has a set type, performing the Set Type command again replaces the set type. To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. | Any definition element | Any usage element |
| Set Trigger>]]> | Any definition element | Transition |
| Set Payload>]]> | Any definition element | Accept Action |
| Create Flow>]]> | Item def, Attribute def, Part def | Connection |
| Set Performed Action>]]> | Action | Perform Action |
| Set Use Case Included>]]> | Use Case | Include Use Case |
| Set Exhibited State>]]> | State | Exhibit State |
| Set Required Constraint>]]> | Constraint | Require Constraint |
| Set Assumed Constraint>]]> | Constraint | Assume Constraint |
| Set Asserted Constraint>]]> | Constraint | Assert Constraint |
| Set Satisfied Requirement>]]> | Requirement | Satisfy Requirement |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Dragging and dropping elements or element symbols enables an efficient modeling practice. Using the Drag and Drop handler functionality allows you to easily perform feature typing, subsetting, element creation, and other modeling commands. </p><h3>Using the Drag and Drop handler</h3><p>To use the Drag and Drop handler</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ol><li>Select the element(s) in the Containment tree.</li><li>In a view:<ol><li data-uuid="79bc5350-1f89-437d-88c5-5178ea663983">Select an element symbol and in its smart manipulator, click and hold the <strong>Drag and Drop</strong> handler <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_and_drop_handler.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li data-uuid="a22b147e-8389-442e-95e1-49d97d1b62c8">Select element symbols, then click and hold the <strong>Drag and Drop</strong> handler <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_and_drop_handler.png" /></ac:image> displayed next to one of the selected symbols.<br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ad23bda-3eee-4c7a-a876-604aa6d7d064"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dragging and dropping the symbol itself instead of the Drag and Drop handler <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[changes the element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To take advantage of the functionalities provided by the Drag and Drop handler, make sure to drag the handler, not the symbol<span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li></ol></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag and drop the element/symbol on:</p><ol><li data-uuid="34ef9f28-0bfc-47b5-9804-1813127c01f9"><p class="auto-cursor-target">The target element symbol in a view.</p></li><li data-uuid="52c8143b-14dc-4082-84c1-0bda4045e065"><p class="auto-cursor-target">The appropriate field in the Specification panel.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a919802e-8de9-48f5-a232-551e6eb9441a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can only drag an element symbol onto another element symbol in a view or the appropriate field in the Specification panel, never onto an element in the Containment tree. The latter modifies the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Element ownership change" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[element's ownership]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>You can also drag an element symbol from one view and drop it onto an element symbol on another open view. Drag the symbol while holding down the Drag and Drop handler over the target view tab to display the open view.</li><li data-uuid="4d4bfd40-e75f-454d-8294-154efa4aecde">For a list of all fields that can be specified via drag and drop in the Specification panel, see the <ac:link ac:anchor="Modifying additional properties via the Specification panel"><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification panel" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Specification panel]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></li><li>The Drag and Drop selection menu appears. Click the appropriate command in the menu.</li></ol><h3>Drag and Drop handler commands</h3><p>Once you drag and drop an element/symbol onto another symbol, the drag and drop selection menu appears. It displays multiple commands whose availability depends on the source (the dragged element/symbol) and target (the symbol on which the dragged source is dropped). See the table below for available commands based on source and target element symbols. </p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th scope="col">Command name</th><th scope="col">Source</th><th scope="col">Target</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Set Type</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Defining an element via the Drag and Drop handler in a view"><ri:page ri:content-title="Feature typing" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36da9b09-77c1-4a65-9384-34eb3be67cc8"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">If an element already has a set type, performing the <strong>Set Type</strong> command again replaces the set type. <br />To set an additional type, hold the CTRL key while executing the command. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td><td>Any definition element</td><td>Any usage element</td></tr><tr><td><p>Set Trigger</p><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Specifying triggers for transitions via the Drag and Drop handler in a view"><ri:page
````
